name: Luna & Soli Bonta
subject: Sharing the puppy love
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROB BONTA FOR CALIFORNIA ATTORNEY GENERAL 2026 FPPC ID #145642
body:
# [](https://act.robbonta.com/go/3?refcode=em20250214&t=1&akid=1642%2E1088799%2EUqn3oe)
Howl you doing? It’s Luna and Soli Bonta, Rob’s canine companions.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-20250214?refcode=em20250214-1642&amount=5&refcode2=1642_1088799_Uqn3oe&akid=1642%2E1088799%2EUqn3oe)
We’re barking your way because we know things have been ruff out there since a certain pet-less human returned to the White House. Today, we wanted to share some puppy love.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-20250214?refcode=em20250214-1642&amount=5&refcode2=1642_1088799_Uqn3oe&akid=1642%2E1088799%2EUqn3oe)
No bones about it, Rob is pawsitively committed to fighting for all Californians as Attorney General. He’s been so doggone busy filing lawsuits and taking action to hold the Trump administration accountable, so **we’re hoping you can help support our best fur-iend this Valentine’s Day.**
**[Will you paw-lease pitch in $5 or any amount that’s meaningful to you to support Rob’s people (and puppy) powered campaign?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-20250214?refcode=em20250214-1642&amount=5&refcode2=1642_1088799_Uqn3oe&akid=1642%2E1088799%2EUqn3oe)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-20250214?refcode=em20250214-1642&amount=5&refcode2=1642_1088799_Uqn3oe&akid=1642%2E1088799%2EUqn3oe)**
Woof you to the moon and back!
— Luna and Soli 🐾

clean_body: Howl you doing? It’s Luna and Soli Bonta, Rob’s canine companions. We’re barking your way because we know things have been ruff out there since a certain pet-less human returned to the White House. Today, we wanted to share some puppy love. No bones about it, Rob is pawsitively committed to fighting for all Californians as Attorney General. He’s been so doggone busy filing lawsuits and taking action to hold the Trump administration accountable, so we’re hoping you can help support our best fur-iend this Valentine’s Day. Woof you to the moon and back! — Luna and Soli 🐾

name: Senator Jennifer Boysko
subject: Boysko Bulletin: Happy Valentine's Day!
body:
| | |
---
**Boysko Bulletin: Happy Valentine's Day!**
---
Dear Peter, Happy Valentine’s Day! We’ve had another busy couple of weeks - stormy weather, bills moving through the House, Committee meetings, meetings with advocates, and passage of the Senate version of the budget. My two top priorities are on track: the constitutional amendment I carried to add reproductive freedom to the Virginia Constitution has passed this session. We need to pass it again after next November’s election and then all Virginia voters will have the opportunity to vote on the amendment. To be successful pro-choice Democrats MUST win the majority in the House of Delegates elections in the fall. Another top priority has been to stop the casino referendum bill that would place it in or near the Tyson’s area. The bill died in a House Appropriations subcommittee this week. There are rumors that it could be brought back - I will continue to fight against all efforts to bring a casino to or near to District 38. Please continue to let your legislators know how you feel. I am pleased that several of my budget amendments were adopted by the Senate including $3.3 million for WMATA, $223 million to lift the support cap for school staff - finally able to raise their pay, and $186,000 for Specially Adapted Resource Clubs, $686,000 for provider capacity for DD Waiver Services, and $125,000 to buy firearms safes for the poorest Virginians. [The Commonwealth Institute side by side comparison of the Senate, House and Governor’s versions of the biennial budget](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102154262/528266244/1053718912?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOGJiOTVlYTQtMzFlYi1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5NGY0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=dKpI6wwF7bvYpZ0oBWkN8ho09CVGWBefxrTSX3X_-gA=&emci=9192a5ab-a5e4-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=8bb95ea4-31eb-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=22486556) shows clearly where our priorities lie. I’ve heard from many friends far and near over the past few days regarding the consequences for speaking out against the casino proposed for the Tyson’s area. While I am very proud of the work I’ve done as a budget conferee, Chair of the Senate Transportation Committee and member of the Rules Committee, I was elected to represent my community. To lose those leadership positions was a harsh penalty. But I do not regret standing up for our community. As a whole, thousands of people have worked together to defeat the casino legislation and I am proud to have led that effort. Sometimes even members of the same party do not think as one. We must continue to work together to fight for our values. I hope that you have a special Valentine’s Day. I’m headed back to NOVA to spend time with my family! Kindest regards,
---
---
Jennifer B. Boysko Member, Senate of Virginia Representing the newly created 38th Senate District making up the northernmost portions of Fairfax County (Great Falls, Herndon, McLean, Reston, WolfTrap, parts of unincorporated Vienna and a slice of Falls Church) **Photo Gallery**
---
---
SPCA Dogs rescued from California wildfires greeted us on Valentine's Day.

Meeting with members of Justice Forward Virginia. **Legislative Updates** **Senator Boysko’s 2025 Legislative Slate** **SJ247 Constitutional amendment; fundamental right to reproductive freedom (first reference):** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1002 Campaign finance; prohibited personal use of campaign funds; complaints, hearings, civil penalty, and advisory opinions:** Passed Senate and on the House Floor **SB1004 Trailer franchise requirements; applicability to certain trailers** : Passed the General Assembly! **SB1005 Services for sexual assault patients; provision of information for sexual assault patients; Task Force on Services for Survivors of Sexual Assault; work group; report** : Passed the General Assembly! **SB1013 Affirmative defense or reduced penalty for a neurocognitive disorder or intellectual or developmental disability** : Passed the Senate; on the House Floor **SB1122 Paid family and medical leave insurance program; notice requirements; civil action** : Passed the Senate; failed in the House after it was brought to the Floor as a revenue bill requiring a 2/3's vote. While frustrating, I am glad that Delegate Sewell's identical bill passed in both the House and the Senate. **SB1123 Extreme Weather Relief Act established:** passed by in the Senate Agriculture, Conservation and Natural Resources Committee with a letter for further study **SB1124 Board of Education; driver education programs; computer-based driver education courses; requirements** : Passed the Senate; reported from House K-12 Education Subcommittee to full Education Committee **SB1125 Department of Wildlife Resources; propagation of wildlife without a permit unlawful; premature separation; hybridization:** Passed the Senate; on the House Floor **SB1126 Students with individualized education programs or Section 504 Plans; emergency protocol and guide:** killed in the full Senate Education and Health Committee **SB1127 Animal testing facilities; public notification:** Passed the Senate; on the House Floor **SB1129 National Teacher Certification Incentive Reward Program and Fund; eligibility; incentive grant awards:** reported from the Senate Education and Health Committee and killed in the Senate Finance and Appropriations Committee. **SB1132 Prohibiting employer seeking wage or salary history of prospective employees; wage or salary range transparency; cause of action:** Passed the Senate; on the House Floor **SB1133 Restaurants and catering operations; notice of food containing pork products; civil penalty:** Passed the Senate; killed in the House Health Subcommittee of the Committee on Health and Human Services **SB1134 Storage of firearms in a residence where a minor or person prohibited from possessing a firearm is present; penalty:** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1135 Board of Pharmacy; regulation of COMP360:** Passed the Senate; on the House Floor Monday **SB1136 Local anti-rent gouging authority; civil penalty:** killed by the Senate General Laws Committee **SB1137 Office of Data Governance and Analytics; oversight; Chief Data Officer; duties:** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1237 Department of Medical Assistance Services; appeals of agency determinations (Admin Bill):** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1483 Regulated hemp product retail facility registration; fee:** passed the Senate; killed by the Agriculture Subcommittee of the House Agriculture, Chesapeake and Natural Resources Committee **How to Track Legislation** You can follow Senator Boysko’s legislation [**here**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102154263/528266245/785536963?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOGJiOTVlYTQtMzFlYi1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5NGY0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=dKpI6wwF7bvYpZ0oBWkN8ho09CVGWBefxrTSX3X_-gA=&emci=9192a5ab-a5e4-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=8bb95ea4-31eb-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=22486556)**.** By selecting “Delegates and Senators” and then my name, you will find my page. Committee pages are where you will find when committees meet, their dockets, and what bills the committees are considering. The page for each bill lists the committee referral and there is a drop down menu that takes you to the committee page to figure out when the bill will be presented. We often get short notice. If you support one or more of my bills, email me at SenatorBoysko@senate.virginia.gov and we will add you to our stakeholder list and notify you when the bill(s) will be heard. Your support is very important to the success of my legislation.

Tuition details and financial aid applications are available [online](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102154276/528266285/-378921988?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOGJiOTVlYTQtMzFlYi1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5NGY0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=dKpI6wwF7bvYpZ0oBWkN8ho09CVGWBefxrTSX3X_-gA=&emci=9192a5ab-a5e4-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=8bb95ea4-31eb-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=22486556).

clean_body: | | | Boysko Bulletin: Happy Valentine's Day! Dear Peter, Happy Valentine’s Day! We’ve had another busy couple of weeks - stormy weather, bills moving through the House, Committee meetings, meetings with advocates, and passage of the Senate version of the budget. My two top priorities are on track: the constitutional amendment I carried to add reproductive freedom to the Virginia Constitution has passed this session. We need to pass it again after next November’s election and then all Virginia voters will have the opportunity to vote on the amendment. To be successful pro-choice Democrats MUST win the majority in the House of Delegates elections in the fall. Another top priority has been to stop the casino referendum bill that would place it in or near the Tyson’s area. The bill died in a House Appropriations subcommittee this week. There are rumors that it could be brought back - I will continue to fight against all efforts to bring a casino to or near to District 38. Please continue to let your legislators know how you feel. I am pleased that several of my budget amendments were adopted by the Senate including $3.3 million for WMATA, $223 million to lift the support cap for school staff - finally able to raise their pay, and $186,000 for Specially Adapted Resource Clubs, $686,000 for provider capacity for DD Waiver Services, and $125,000 to buy firearms safes for the poorest Virginians. shows clearly where our priorities lie. I’ve heard from many friends far and near over the past few days regarding the consequences for speaking out against the casino proposed for the Tyson’s area. While I am very proud of the work I’ve done as a budget conferee, Chair of the Senate Transportation Committee and member of the Rules Committee, I was elected to represent my community. To lose those leadership positions was a harsh penalty. But I do not regret standing up for our community. As a whole, thousands of people have worked together to defeat the casino legislation and I am proud to have led that effort. Sometimes even members of the same party do not think as one. We must continue to work together to fight for our values. I hope that you have a special Valentine’s Day. I’m headed back to NOVA to spend time with my family! Kindest regards, Jennifer B. Boysko Member, Senate of Virginia Representing the newly created 38th Senate District making up the northernmost portions of Fairfax County (Great Falls, Herndon, McLean, Reston, WolfTrap, parts of unincorporated Vienna and a slice of Falls Church) Photo Gallery SPCA Dogs rescued from California wildfires greeted us on Valentine's Day. Receiving Dog Kisses from rescues from the LA fires with Senator Stanley, another animal advocate. Receiving a large packet of valentines from ReproRising and their supporters across the state. Valentine's Day started off well with a special visit from my constituent and First Lady of Virginia, Suzanne S. Youngkin. Over 90% of Democrats and Republicans support access to contraception, so I was glad to join my colleagues at the Repro Rising Contraceptive Access Press Conference on Thursday where I spoke to the need to safeguard our freedom to this form of health care. On Monday, February 10th I was happy to present SB1013 and see it pass through the House Criminal Subcommittee and again today through the full Courts of Justice Committee. My limitation on personal use of campaign funds passed the Senate with strong bipartisan support! Thanks to all of the stakeholders who have worked on this for years and a special note of appreciation to Delegate Marcus Simon who started the effort years ago. I was disappointed to see SB982, the casino bill pass out of the Senate and head to the House, but pleased that a House Appropriations Subcommittee later killed the bill. I’m thrilled to have bipartisan support for my bill that gives our residents with intellectual or developmental disabilities protections! I’ve been working on this issue for 5 years. With Freeda the IUD and Senator Srinivasan at the Right to Contraception Act press conference. Meeting with members of the Center for Gun Violence Solutions, Johns Hopkins School of Public Health Meeting with advocates from SEIU and National Coalition of 100 Black Women. Members of NCBW work as change agents to influence policy that promotes gender equity in health, education and economic empowerment. Through their efforts they fulfill their vision of seeing black women and girls living in a world where socio-economic inequity does not exist. Meeting with members of Justice Forward Virginia. Justice Forward Virginia is a non-partisan advocacy organization created to bring attention to the urgent need for criminal justice reform in the Commonwealth—at the most fundamental level—and to advance legislation and advocate for comprehensive changes to Virginia’s criminal legal system. Meeting with Fairfax County At-Large School Board member Ilryong Moon on priorities for our incredible FCPS Schools. Hearing from members of Virginia AARP on three bills they support to lower the costs of prescriptions and protect consumers. Learning about LEGO and their investments in our Commonwealth while also supporting the Blue Brick Wall in LEGO form. Hearing from the Virginia Community Health Worker Association (VACHWA) who provide a space for community health workers (inclusive of all titles) to network and grow personally and professionally through education and advocacy. We also met with constituents Douglas and Mary who have benefited from the good work of VACHWA. Legislative Updates Senator Boysko’s 2025 Legislative Slate SJ247 Constitutional amendment; fundamental right to reproductive freedom (first reference): Passed the General Assembly! SB1002 Campaign finance; prohibited personal use of campaign funds; complaints, hearings, civil penalty, and advisory opinions: Passed Senate and on the House Floor SB1004 Trailer franchise requirements; applicability to certain trailers : Passed the General Assembly! SB1005 Services for sexual assault patients; provision of information for sexual assault patients; Task Force on Services for Survivors of Sexual Assault; work group; report : Passed the General Assembly! SB1013 Affirmative defense or reduced penalty for a neurocognitive disorder or intellectual or developmental disability : Passed the Senate; on the House Floor SB1122 Paid family and medical leave insurance program; notice requirements; civil action : Passed the Senate; failed in the House after it was brought to the Floor as a revenue bill requiring a 2/3's vote. While frustrating, I am glad that Delegate Sewell's identical bill passed in both the House and the Senate. SB1123 Extreme Weather Relief Act established: passed by in the Senate Agriculture, Conservation and Natural Resources Committee with a letter for further study SB1124 Board of Education; driver education programs; computer-based driver education courses; requirements : Passed the Senate; reported from House K-12 Education Subcommittee to full Education Committee SB1125 Department of Wildlife Resources; propagation of wildlife without a permit unlawful; premature separation; hybridization: Passed the Senate; on the House Floor SB1126 Students with individualized education programs or Section 504 Plans; emergency protocol and guide: killed in the full Senate Education and Health Committee SB1127 Animal testing facilities; public notification: Passed the Senate; on the House Floor SB1129 National Teacher Certification Incentive Reward Program and Fund; eligibility; incentive grant awards: reported from the Senate Education and Health Committee and killed in the Senate Finance and Appropriations Committee. SB1132 Prohibiting employer seeking wage or salary history of prospective employees; wage or salary range transparency; cause of action: Passed the Senate; on the House Floor SB1133 Restaurants and catering operations; notice of food containing pork products; civil penalty: Passed the Senate; killed in the House Health Subcommittee of the Committee on Health and Human Services SB1134 Storage of firearms in a residence where a minor or person prohibited from possessing a firearm is present; penalty: Passed the General Assembly! SB1135 Board of Pharmacy; regulation of COMP360: Passed the Senate; on the House Floor Monday SB1136 Local anti-rent gouging authority; civil penalty: killed by the Senate General Laws Committee SB1137 Office of Data Governance and Analytics; oversight; Chief Data Officer; duties: Passed the General Assembly! SB1237 Department of Medical Assistance Services; appeals of agency determinations (Admin Bill): Passed the General Assembly! SB1483 Regulated hemp product retail facility registration; fee: passed the Senate; killed by the Agriculture Subcommittee of the House Agriculture, Chesapeake and Natural Resources Committee How to Track Legislation You can follow Senator Boysko’s legislation . By selecting “Delegates and Senators” and then my name, you will find my page. You will see the committees I serve on and my Chief Patron, Chief Co-patron and Co-patron legislation. Committee pages are where you will find when committees meet, their dockets, and what bills the committees are considering. The page for each bill lists the committee referral and there is a drop down menu that takes you to the committee page to figure out when the bill will be presented. We often get short notice. If you support one or more of my bills, email me at SenatorBoysko@senate.virginia.gov and we will add you to our stakeholder list and notify you when the bill(s) will be heard. Your support is very important to the success of my legislation. | | Constituent Services Addiction and Recovery Council As a member of the , we work together to ensure that people have the resources they need to identify and get help for addiction recovery.Addiction is a life threatening problem for too many Virginians. Please reach out if you need help for yourself or a loved one. Reach Out for Mental Health Support The is available nationwide. You can also find support for yourself and family members by reaching out to or. The Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration National Helpline: 1-800-662-4357 National Drug Helpline: 1-844-289-0879 National Institute of Mental Health Information Resource Center: 1-866-615-6464 Partnership for Drug-Free Kids: 1-855-378-4373 Unemployment Assistance and Resources for Job Searchers For those eligible for benefits, the Virginia Employment Commission (VEC) requires that people receiving unemployment benefits show proof of job searches each week. I encourage you to seek support for finding a job from . They help identify training programs, job opportunities, and help with resumes, emails, and interviewing skills. My constituents report good results working with VCW. . Help Paying Bills If you are struggling to pay bills, please visit Virginia’s ** to figure out if you qualify for assistance. You can also reach out to your county - your local government will review your financial situation and connect you with resources. In Fairfax County, contact . are additional resources for food. If you have lost your health insurance, please visit to figure out if you are eligible for Medicaid or for help with Affordable Care Act insurance. Support for Our Immigrant Community Members I will continue to work to ensure that our communities are welcoming, diverse and provide opportunities for all people to live with dignity and safety. Working together, we can build good lives for our families and our communities. The news of ICE sweeps is distressing. We can help by sharing information with our neighbors regarding their rights. Please share this information from the ACLU, available in multiple languages: We are also working with the Legal Aid Justice Center which has asked that we share their . Our community is better when we embrace our diversity and keep each other safe! Opportunities Apply for the Sorensen Institute for Political Leadership High School Leaders Program The University of Virginia is now accepting applications for its Sorensen Institute High School Leaders Program. Participants learn how Virginia government, both state and local, functions, how public policy is shaped and how to communicate effectively. This program prepares future leaders for ethical and responsible civic engagement and public service in the Commonwealth of Virginia. Applications for this in-person summer program, July 12 - 26, 2025, are due by March 17, 2025 with supplemental materials due by March 21, 2025. Tuition details and financial aid applications are available . Give Back REPRO Rising Virginia is working to ensure that everyone has access to abortion care and reproductive healthcare. I've been working with them for many years, from their time as NARAL Virginia to now. Their support has been critical for passing the constitutional amendment for reproductive freedom! In the News Events Saturdays, year round, 8 am- noon (May – Oct), 9 am – noon (Nov – April)/, located at the Virginia Village Shopping Center, 30 Catoctin Circle, SE, in Leesburg We welcome notices about events that are free or very low cost. Keep in Touch** I am now in Richmond for the 2025 General Assembly Session. My team includes my Chief of Staff Karen Harrison, and aides, Kathy Shupe, Gillie Cuda, and Juliet Morro. Team Boysko also includes Ava Kegler from Hollins University, and Olga Rokhlenko and Jackson Sanders-Bussey from the University of Richmond. For constituent services and to schedule meetings with your community group, please call (703) 437-0086 or email Senator Boysko@senate.virginia.gov. To schedule a meeting in Richmond, you can send an email or call 804-698-7538. Friend me on and follow me on | | If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe. Friends of Jennifer Boysko PO Box 247 Herndon, VA 20172 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please .

body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102155359/528276595/577006200?amount=25&refcode=header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMDk5MDQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZTkxYjFlODAtMzRlYi1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5NGY0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=AWCZLn4UMNTtjLx3SdAGgTZUI90CWBqj3mC529X-naw=)
Peter,

You’ve probably seen the headlines by now. Yes, I swore when talking about Donald Trump. And no, I’m not sorry.

If anything, I regret not saying it with more conviction—because the truth is, Donald Trump is a clear and present danger to our democracy, our freedoms, and our basic American values.

While MAGA extremists obsess over my language, I’m focused on the real obscenity:
* Trump has handed power to Elon Musk, an unelected billionaire who is meddling in federal agencies and doing untold damage.
* Trump is trampling on our Constitution.
* Trump is waging a war of hate on our immigrant communities.
* And he’s just getting started.

But here’s the thing—taking on Trump and his enablers isn’t just about calling them out. It’s about defeating them at the ballot box. That’s where you come in.
I need you to stand with me to show that Democrats are fighting back and ready to take back the Majority in the House.
****[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102155360/528276596/577006200?amount=25&refcode=MD_FR_2024.12.10_OTC1_B1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMDk5MDQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZTkxYjFlODAtMzRlYi1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5NGY0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=AWCZLn4UMNTtjLx3SdAGgTZUI90CWBqj3mC529X-naw=)****
---
If you agree that we need fearless leadership—not just in our words but in our actions—chip in today to help me fight back.

I won’t apologize for speaking up. And I won’t stop fighting
Let's go,
Maxine

name: The Squad
subject: Our Squad leads with radical love💖
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20250214-1228-foote&t=1001&akid=1228%2E342411%2EVdsFAF ]Squad Victory Fund

Peter,

Alexandria, Ilhan, Rashida, and Ayanna lead with radical love. 💘 They work
tirelessly to center humanity, healing, and justice, and they’re making
incredible strides in building a truly inclusive, multi-racial democracy
where we all belong.

That’s why this Valentine's Day, we want to send them a special gift: a
digital card signed by thousands of supporters across the country (like
YOU!) to show them just how much we love and appreciate their leadership.
💌✨

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/signup/wish-squad-happy-valentines-day/?source=em20250214-1228&akid=1228%2E342411%2EVdsFAF ]Peter, will
you add your name to our special Valentine’s Day card before we share it
with Alexandria, Ilhan, Rashida, and Ayanna?

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/signup/wish-squad-happy-valentines-day/?source=em20250214-1228&akid=1228%2E342411%2EVdsFAF ]A picture of Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Rashida Tlaib, Ayanna Pressley,
and Ilhan Omar in front of a pink and purple background with text that
reads: 'WISH THE SQUAD A HAPPY VALENTINES DAY! To: AOC, Ilhan, Ayanna, &
Rashida. From: Everyone in our squad'. They are surrounded by four heart
candies that read 'TAX THE RICH, CLEAN AIR, M4A, GND'. There is a button
at the bottom that reads 'SIGN THE CARD'.

Together, we’ll show just how BIG our movement is and how much we cherish
their work advancing racial, social, economic, and environmental justice.

Thank you for being part of this team,
Peter. Wishing you a
Valentine’s Day full of love, joy, and solidarity! 💖🌸

Together,

Squad Victory HQ

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.squadvictoryfund.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1228%2E342411%2EVdsFAF

Paid for by The Squad Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated as follows: 8.33% to each of Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, Rooted in Community Leadership PAC, Politivist PAC, and For the Children PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

name: David Hogg
subject: Seven years ago
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
Seven years ago today was the worst day of my life.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20250214-1&t=1&akid=1740%2E587540%2E0NOesC)
Peter,
Seven years ago today was the worst day of my life. On that day, a 19-year-old who never should’ve had access to a gun in the first place entered my high school with an AR-15 and took the lives of 17 students and faculty members and injured 17 others.
Since then, I’ve worked to get stronger gun laws passed across the country because I never want anyone else to hear the deafening cries of their classmates after their friends have been stolen from them by the epidemic of preventable gun violence.
I’ll be honest, this work is soul-crushing. No one would choose to spend every day talking about the worst day of their life because they want to — but the reason people who’ve experienced this do this is because we feel we have no other choice. Like with all forms of activism, it is not voluntary, it is mandatory to organize for others' survival.
Because despite the empty “thoughts and prayers” offered by too many of our leaders, this continues to happen on a regular basis. Thoughts and prayers aren’t enough on their own — they need to be paired with action.
Though this work is incredibly hard, it is working. President Biden created the first ever Office of Gun Violence Prevention which helped act like FEMA for instances of gun violence and mass shootings along with making sure the hundreds of millions allocated by Congress to combat gun violence was actually used. The other day I was talking with one of the former directors of the gun violence prevention office, who told me in 2023 we saw the single largest one year reduction in gun homicides in US history of 12% and the data for 2024 are pointing to an additional 15% reduction. That means we cut gun homicides by nearly a third in just two years. That means thousands are alive right now who otherwise would not be. 1⁄3 is nowhere near enough but it’s a hell of a lot better than where we were headed. I spent many days of college in between classes on zoom calls with the White House working to create that office and had worked on the idea since introducing it with March For Our Lives in 2019. What keeps me going are my incredible family, friends, fellow survivors and knowing what we are doing is working, even if it doesn’t feel like it sometimes.
One day, I want to live in a world where no one ever has to experience the pain so many experienced that day in Parkland and every day in America. But until that day is a reality, we have to keep doing this work. It’s too important not to. My generation knows that if we don’t have a government that will change gun laws then we’ll change who’s in government. You are what makes that possible. I deeply appreciate you and your commitment to electing the next generation at a time when it is so easy to feel hopeless. We will keep fighting like hell and we will succeed.
David Hogg
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=2&akid=1740%2E587540%2E0NOesC)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=3&akid=1740%2E587540%2E0NOesC)

To give by check:
Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003
Email us: [info@leaderswedeserve.com](mailto:info@leaderswedeserve.com)
PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE.COM, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE

name: Justice Democrats
subject: Trump and Musk are attacking civil rights, full stop.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

Donald Trump and unelected billionaire Elon Musk have taken an axe to
government civil rights and equity programs – calling everything they
don’t like DEI and ending federal initiatives to promote diversity,
equity, and inclusion in the government.

But they didn’t stop there:

* Trump signed executive orders banning transgender and nonbinary people
from serving in the armed forces and participating in girls’ and
women’s sports;
* Words like gender, transgender, nonbinary, and anything having to do
with racial justice have been purged from federal government websites;
* Google Calendar has followed suit and removed cultural events like
Pride Month, Black History Month, Holocaust Remembrance Day, and more.

Make no mistake: This administration is using DEI as a catch-all to attack
everyone’s civil rights, reward inequality, and ensure no one stands in
the way of them making unlimited profits for their businesses and donors.

Fueled by the far-right’s white nationalism and corporate greed, the
Trump-Musk administration wants to build a government that does not
reflect America at the expense of everyday Americans.

We’re not going to let them get away with this bigotry without a fight.
Justice Democrats is proud to have elected a diverse, working-class bloc
of progressives in Congress who are uniting all communities against the
corruption of this Trump administration.

But in order to build a country that truly represents and serves everyone,
we have to build our people power in Congress.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250214?refcode=em20250214-36433&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36433_1203867_FLhYKu&akid=36433%2E1203867%2EFLhYKu ]We’re currently recruiting working-class, progressive candidates to run
for Congress in 2026. Can you pitch in $5 today to help us
elect bold leaders who fill stand up for civil rights and fight back
against Trump and Musk’s bigotry?

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250214?refcode=em20250214-36433&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36433_1203867_FLhYKu&akid=36433%2E1203867%2EFLhYKu ]Contribute $5

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250214-36433-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36433_1203867_FLhYKu&akid=36433%2E1203867%2EFLhYKu

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. name: Team Schrier
subject: Foreign billionaires like Musk don't get to call the shots for the American people
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress
body:
House Speaker Mike Johnson thinks what Elon Musk is doing is just fine. But let’s be clear: It couldn't be more reckless. [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102155861/528290051/693975070?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3OWFhMmQzOC0zOGViLWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JiNUVxJl87TLqGilXoqO12wCcM96V9ldliZLUdJ2qXg=&emci=085d58f8-39ea-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=79aa2d38-38eb-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=15820338)
---
Peter –

House Speaker Mike Johnson thinks what Elon Musk is doing is just fine. But let’s be clear: **It couldn't be more reckless.**

No one elected Musk to public office. Yet, thanks to Republican leadership enabling him at every turn, he’s pushing the limits of executive power, influencing national security decisions, and putting his own interests above what’s best for the American people.

And instead of holding him accountable, Speaker Mike Johnson and his GOP allies are standing back and letting it happen. Just look at what’s happening with the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) – _the newly created executive office Musk is opaquely operating behind closed doors._

The White House’s own executive order made it clear that DOGE wouldn’t have access to classified information, yet Musk has been making sweeping claims about what the U.S. government is doing abroad and hinting at privileged knowledge. That’s not just absurd – it’s dangerous.

**But the problem isn’t just Musk.**

It’s that Republican leaders like Johnson are allowing wealthy elites and unelected billionaires to call the shots instead of listening to the American people. And if we don’t act now, they’ll keep handing more and more power to the ultra-rich while working families in Washington’s 8th District – and across the country – get left behind. Kim is fighting every day to make sure Congress works for the people – not for billionaires looking to expand their influence. But to keep up this fight, we need your help.

[**Will you rush a donation of $10 or more today to help Kim hold these extremists accountable and ensure that WA-08 has a leader who will always put the people – _and their privacy_ – first? It’s that Republican leaders like Johnson are allowing wealthy elites and unelected billionaires to call the shots instead of listening to the American people. And if we don’t act now, they’ll keep handing more and more power to the ultra-rich while working families in Washington’s 8th District – and across the country – get left behind. Kim is fighting every day to make sure Congress works for the people – not for billionaires looking to expand their influence. But to keep up this fight, we need your help. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you, Team Schrier Rep. Kim Schrier is a doctor, scientist, and mom, fighting for Washington's 8th District in Congress. This is one of the most competitive districts in the country, so we need your help to keep Kim in Congress. Chip in now to help protect Kim's seat. = Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress Contributions or gifts to Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress are not tax deductible. subject: What will you do today, friend?

body:
| Every single Democrat reading this message has a choice to make:

Are we going to settle for tax breaks for the wealthy, the well-off and the well-connected? **Or will we fight to lower the high cost of living in America?**

Will we accept dark money special interests buying power and influence in Washington? **Or will we fight for an economy that works for everyone?**

Will we sit quietly as Donald Trump abuses the power of the presidency? **Or will we fight against corruption and defend our democracy?**

[Team, this is our chance to show the strength and determination of our movement. If YOU choose to fight for democracy, economic opportunity and a revival of the American dream, please split a donation of $10 between Voter Protection Project and the DCCC to help flip the House blue and build unstoppable momentum for Democrats across the country. The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=18935%2E878179%2Edlr0eh). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=18935%2E878179%2Edlr0eh).**

subject: It’s not rocket-science
body:
While you and I have no doubt President Trump will deliver on his promise to secure the border, this threat will not go away. | |
---
| | It isn’t rocket-science. It’s basic math.
---
| |
---
[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=01c1ec4330a2455db49ea697c6f8b6ce&_e=a5Mq31e5CXB8D6Mjn-Mb5R519zW2p7hi2boDOG1348NWEUHTVxCMF_nanV0FMjNO5JT0FaHb0VHKhFGzXsHILjS7tMo2wQDHHYzwQx_vLZlBCHz7BaeRfOXrOT_OeHYSSr72pbljUYgLWHy7TNGXyE4qK7dXIz3b1TfXUPGPcCffNHUvr9ON1FcQ8QQ7yYh39f-e5oeMD1SzGWn05YWV79ZqReGgzxNN0bIh2XYvx0w9lmyH3H_ZW3oghaSO9FeA90UGcIW3NKM5BPTmIwBhb7zSEaUo3Q72B_IP9OWHAyD87kzLtecESC6xwEZ15Bs74ScChcpwNs_yF7D4iblp8RSmL8PtD3mg4EYF5oqMVIA%3D)

| | **“****A disproportionate number of illegal immigrants go to blue states, they amplify the effect of a blue state vote. My understanding is that Democrats would lose approximately 20 seats in the house if illegals were not counted in census. And that’s also 20 less electoral votes for president.**** ** **So, illegals absolutely do impact who controls the House and the presidency.”****~** Elon Musk
---
| | Fellow Conservative,Elon Musk is right. It isn’t rocket-science. It’s basic math. It’s also a ticking time-bomb set by Joe Biden and Kamala Harris – in the form of 11.5 million illegals entering our country on their watch – one which will explode shortly after President Trump leaves office in 2029.While you and I have no doubt President Trump will deliver on his promise to secure the border, this threat will not go away. The truth is, without immediate action, these illegal aliens will be counted in the U.S. Census, which determines both the apportionment of seats in the House of Representatives _and_ the number of electoral votes given to each state. Whether they came because, four years ago, Kamala Harris and Joe Biden prompted them to “surge to the border” for asylum or because of blue-states offering sanctuary status, “free” health care, “free” cell phones, “free” housing, and “free” college...it doesn’t matter. The result is the same.Your vote is _already_ being **watered down** in Congress. Your vote is _already_ being **watered down** in the Electoral College.And every day reports are popping up of non-citizens, illegal aliens, and convicted felons being registered to vote in cities and entire swing-states across the country, which means your vote may be fully **canceled out in future elections**. **_American elections should be decided by American citizens._****[If you agree then please sign your “BAN NON-CITIZEN VOTING” Petition to Amend the U.S. Constitution so the chaos we’re seeing now _never_ happens again in America.](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=01c1ec4330a2455db49ea697c6f8b6ce&_e=a5Mq31e5CXB8D6Mjn-Mb5R519zW2p7hi2boDOG1348NWEUHTVxCMF_nanV0FMjNO5JT0FaHb0VHKhFGzXsHILjS7tMo2wQDHHYzwQx_vLZlBCHz7BaeRfOXrOT_OeHYSSr72pbljUYgLWHy7TNGXyE4qK7dXIz3b1TfXUPGPcCffNHUvr9ON1FcQ8QQ7yYh39f-e5oeMD1SzGWn05YWV773tpOHlF7VPKb32sbkrCE4SUH15F6ESCjktfbUs0hB-S5PHZsHBCyLB0NpB57YCNt-13-2x8ZI2YIz3xGbaTisCtLxdUpUiG9T4B9k6YLKqY7SjVW0AdbAW2dw2TbuNh-iMdtH9uzSDa0SsxddHeM8%3D)**Because, here’s the scary part:The last census took place in 2020. That was _before_ the 11.5 million illegal aliens who invaded our country under Joe Biden and Kamala Harris. To put it in context, there are only 8 states in America with a population over 11 million. So, it’s as if the radical Left just imported the 9th largest state in America. That’s more than North Carolina (10.8 million), Michigan (10 million), New Jersey (9.2 million), or Virginia (8.7 million).You get the picture. That’s why urgent action is needed to pass a Constitutional Amendment to ban illegal aliens from voting and from being counted for purposes of congressional and electoral vote apportionment. The next census in less than 6 years away. If those 11.5 million illegal aliens are counted in the census or are allowed to vote in the intervening years, you and I simply won’t have a country. **[It’s vitally important that your representatives in Washington and in your state legislature hear this demand directly from you.](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=01c1ec4330a2455db49ea697c6f8b6ce&_e=a5Mq31e5CXB8D6Mjn-Mb5R519zW2p7hi2boDOG1348NWEUHTVxCMF_nanV0FMjNO5JT0FaHb0VHKhFGzXsHILjS7tMo2wQDHHYzwQx_vLZlBCHz7BaeRfOXrOT_OeHYSSr72pbljUYgLWHy7TNGXyE4qK7dXIz3b1TfXUPGPcCffNHUvr9ON1FcQ8QQ7yYh39f-e5oeMD1SzGWn05YWV7xt5G4BR-U932garSsJnf41_0A1z0X9sqs57ffchOIOSEj7sqEk855c7cxFTrpvemHpHQ2McvyDDZ5ZFHw5Hg4ArmkDlxL0TMx1Hx31P3LNH2XjDzz984MwcFjuhvQFzVeAlupWeFYWVcguzFXXIEQg%3D)** Because, quite honestly, they think they can pull a fast one on you. They think you and I will be so relieved to see the border secure again and criminal illegal aliens deported, that you and I will be oblivious to this threat lying dormant under our very eyes.No way.My name is Rebecca Weber. I’m the chairwoman of AMAC Action. **AMAC Action is the most influential advocacy group in the country when it comes to standing for the values of conservative Seniors.****We’ve got 72 chapters and 300 volunteer delegates, who are the “feet on the street” advocates who contact Members of Congress and state legislators in their district offices.** **Moreover, our chapters and delegates help organize Town Hall meetings with elected officials and candidates to enable those locally of our 2,000,000 AMAC members to meet, hear, and question them.****And you should also know that we now have more than 337,000 AMAC members who respond to our “calls-to-action” and weigh in with members of the** **U.S. House and Senate, as well as their state legislators on the issues.** Quite simply, when we speak, Washington listens. [](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=01c1ec4330a2455db49ea697c6f8b6ce&_e=a5Mq31e5CXB8D6Mjn-Mb5R519zW2p7hi2boDOG1348MdJY1uzSyvhjSa9lCohcusL4TarNfvuoh10LoEHaTduO9CDt3MQ-GPoIgGvtEFBP7jp4HunS5uLrU71UseG9m8CpW3ji30TfVG8O_wb_hb1Eb8PcqAMhBjOKuwPz7c26maEyBBNUbJSqnSHNxgYbOduqlAR7yW8CTg6gTxM_z6xr3rJO8z92g35kknalOlCPw%3D)**[And AMAC Action needs your help today in this do-or-die battle to pass a Constitutional Amendment to protect your vote from being canceled out by an illegal alien or a convicted felon.](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=01c1ec4330a2455db49ea697c6f8b6ce&_e=a5Mq31e5CXB8D6Mjn-Mb5R519zW2p7hi2boDOG1348NWEUHTVxCMF_nanV0FMjNO5JT0FaHb0VHKhFGzXsHILjS7tMo2wQDHHYzwQx_vLZlBCHz7BaeRfOXrOT_OeHYSSr72pbljUYgLWHy7TNGXyE4qK7dXIz3b1TfXUPGPcCffNHUvr9ON1FcQ8QQ7yYh39f-e5oeMD1SzGWn05YWV7xU9leJFABpHZ4odGIiisM6UsMyav_PDCA46fr06sMuXOGHYXFlnUUMh8OwcMXgsD7XViQEysZrQZOtY6P2gPe34i2HGgT7xYg7yLohrsGsD0BQr08eOcxC1szgX2lkEeiFVp5zM2Tl8dYruMy3bX1c%3D)** But I can’t do it without you. **No matter what issue you care about the most, if you and I don’t have a secure,_honest_ elections... none of it matters.**Inflation. Illegal immigration. Violent Crime. Drug use. Homelessness. The 2nd Amendment. Out of control spending. Social Security and Medicare insolvency. Right to Life. Indoctrination in our schools. Endless foreign wars. * **TOP NEWS**

"**Measles outbreak is Texas' largest in 30 years,** " Dallas Morning News'**Emily Brindley** -- "The ongoing measles outbreak in Texas' South Plains region is now the largest the state has seen in more than three decades.

The Texas Department of State Health Services reported on Friday there are now [48 identified measles cases](https://substack.com/redirect/b86d76ba-742c-4c48-acc0-8dfdafa1db19?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), largely among children. The case count includes 13 hospitalizations.

The vast majority of the cases are from residents of Gaines County about 350 miles west of Dallas, where the outbreak appears to have originated. But there are now additional cases identified in the nearby counties of Lynn, Terry and Yoakum.

**Lara Anton** , a spokesperson for the state health department, said the outbreak appears to have originated in a Mennonite community in Gaines County. The Mennonite church is not widely opposed to vaccination. Mennonite leaders have talked publicly about their COVID vaccinations, for instance, and numerous leaders did not support religious exemptions for COVID vaccines.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/4b888623-d39a-4617-90a4-2b28b9409c6d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
All of the confirmed cases are among people who are either unvaccinated or whose vaccination status was unknown, according to the state health department.

Measles is so contagious that during an outbreak "it's very hard to keep it from spreading in a community that has low vaccination rates," Anton said.

The case count now surpasses the number of cases seen in a 2013 outbreak. In that outbreak, a person returned from a trip to Asia with the disease, and the cases spread [through a Tarrant County megachurch](https://substack.com/redirect/7e07a3e1-a692-4399-90be-d1ff33f714e8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). In total, Texas saw 27 measles cases in 2013, which was the highest yearly count in the state in two decades.

The South Plains outbreak already has a higher case count than the state saw in all of 2013. Anton said the case count in the ongoing outbreak may still increase as more cases are identified in the coming days and weeks.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/4b888623-d39a-4617-90a4-2b28b9409c6d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The state health department is urging Texans to get their measles vaccination if they have not already.

The two-dose vaccine regimen offers about [97% protection](https://substack.com/redirect/0eef2b35-8663-449b-8b98-343bd53f7abd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) against the highly contagious virus. People who are not vaccinated are highly likely to contract the virus if they are exposed to it.

Measles is one of the most contagious viruses. The virus can live in the air and on surfaces for [up to two hours](https://substack.com/redirect/3f19c0a2-0077-4335-839a-9e8b82312f4d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) after an infected person has left -- meaning that unvaccinated people in particular can catch the illness even without being in the same room as an infected person." Lara Anton , a spokesperson for the state health department, said the outbreak appears to have originated in a Mennonite community in Gaines County. The Mennonite church is not widely opposed to vaccination. Mennonite leaders have talked publicly about their COVID vaccinations, for instance, and numerous leaders did not support religious exemptions for COVID vaccines. All of the confirmed cases are among people who are either unvaccinated or whose vaccination status was unknown, according to the state health department. Measles is so contagious that during an outbreak "it's very hard to keep it from spreading in a community that has low vaccination rates," Anton said. The case count now surpasses the number of cases seen in a 2013 outbreak. In that outbreak, a person returned from a trip to Asia with the disease, and the cases spread . In total, Texas saw 27 measles cases in 2013, which was the highest yearly count in the state in two decades. The South Plains outbreak already has a higher case count than the state saw in all of 2013. Anton said the case count in the ongoing outbreak may still increase as more cases are identified in the coming days and weeks. The state health department is urging Texans to get their measles vaccination if they have not already. The two-dose vaccine regimen offers about against the highly contagious virus. People who are not vaccinated are highly likely to contract the virus if they are exposed to it. Measles is one of the most contagious viruses. The virus can live in the air and on surfaces for after an infected person has left -- meaning that unvaccinated people in particular can catch the illness even without being in the same room as an infected person." * * *
* **TOP NEWS**

"**Measles outbreak is Texas' largest in 30 years,** " Dallas Morning News'**Emily Brindley** -- "The ongoing measles outbreak in Texas' South Plains region is now the largest the state has seen in more than three decades.

The Texas Department of State Health Services reported on Friday there are now [48 identified measles cases](https://substack.com/redirect/9aa09c1a-1c83-4dda-96ea-d72e0df75bc1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), largely among children. The case count includes 13 hospitalizations.

The vast majority of the cases are from residents of Gaines County about 350 miles west of Dallas, where the outbreak appears to have originated. But there are now additional cases identified in the nearby counties of Lynn, Terry and Yoakum.

**Lara Anton** , a spokesperson for the state health department, said the outbreak appears to have originated in a Mennonite community in Gaines County. The Mennonite church is not widely opposed to vaccination. Mennonite leaders have talked publicly about their COVID vaccinations, for instance, and numerous leaders did not support religious exemptions for COVID vaccines.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/da0d0bff-620b-4730-a4b6-2949c64b1533?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
All of the confirmed cases are among people who are either unvaccinated or whose vaccination status was unknown, according to the state health department.

Measles is so contagious that during an outbreak "it's very hard to keep it from spreading in a community that has low vaccination rates," Anton said.

The case count now surpasses the number of cases seen in a 2013 outbreak. In that outbreak, a person returned from a trip to Asia with the disease, and the cases spread [through a Tarrant County megachurch](https://substack.com/redirect/3db836ae-35b8-4f9f-a47c-f48a8075a47f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). In total, Texas saw 27 measles cases in 2013, which was the highest yearly count in the state in two decades.

The South Plains outbreak already has a higher case count than the state saw in all of 2013. Anton said the case count in the ongoing outbreak may still increase as more cases are identified in the coming days and weeks.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/da0d0bff-620b-4730-a4b6-2949c64b1533?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The state health department is urging Texans to get their measles vaccination if they have not already.

The two-dose vaccine regimen offers about [97% protection](https://substack.com/redirect/4cf987fa-7918-4d8f-a6f9-c3843924a7f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) against the highly contagious virus. People who are not vaccinated are highly likely to contract the virus if they are exposed to it.

Measles is one of the most contagious viruses. The virus can live in the air and on surfaces for [up to two hours](https://substack.com/redirect/12828cd5-5089-4c46-bfdd-84d9d4b99529?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) after an infected person has left -- meaning that unvaccinated people in particular can catch the illness even without being in the same room as an infected person." TOP NEWS "Measles outbreak is Texas' largest in 30 years, " Dallas Morning News'Emily Brindley -- "The ongoing measles outbreak in Texas' South Plains region is now the largest the state has seen in more than three decades. The Texas Department of State Health Services reported on Friday there are now , largely among children. The case count includes 13 hospitalizations. The vast majority of the cases are from residents of Gaines County about 350 miles west of Dallas, where the outbreak appears to have originated. But there are now additional cases identified in the nearby counties of Lynn, Terry and Yoakum. Lara Anton , a spokesperson for the state health department, said the outbreak appears to have originated in a Mennonite community in Gaines County. The Mennonite church is not widely opposed to vaccination. Mennonite leaders have talked publicly about their COVID vaccinations, for instance, and numerous leaders did not support religious exemptions for COVID vaccines. All of the confirmed cases are among people who are either unvaccinated or whose vaccination status was unknown, according to the state health department. Measles is so contagious that during an outbreak "it's very hard to keep it from spreading in a community that has low vaccination rates," Anton said. The case count now surpasses the number of cases seen in a 2013 outbreak. In that outbreak, a person returned from a trip to Asia with the disease, and the cases spread . In total, Texas saw 27 measles cases in 2013, which was the highest yearly count in the state in two decades. The South Plains outbreak already has a higher case count than the state saw in all of 2013. Anton said the case count in the ongoing outbreak may still increase as more cases are identified in the coming days and weeks. The state health department is urging Texans to get their measles vaccination if they have not already. The two-dose vaccine regimen offers about against the highly contagious virus. People who are not vaccinated are highly likely to contract the virus if they are exposed to it. Measles is one of the most contagious viruses. The virus can live in the air and on surfaces for after an infected person has left -- meaning that unvaccinated people in particular can catch the illness even without being in the same room as an infected person." * **TOP NEWS**

“**Measles outbreak is Texas ' largest in 30 years,**” Dallas Morning News'**Emily Brindley** — “The ongoing measles outbreak in Texas’ South Plains region is now the largest the state has seen in more than three decades.

The Texas Department of State Health Services reported on Friday there are now [48 identified measles cases](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eHsB1jlrVb0PxTSyHRKb3PQqoB7q72is9ApNwlHcwxc1qb1dKOxsficwULdCYHJBHIGRAonu8BHxih7r-Q5jozYhcjRYzHkI1V-OCilAAIFOVswcTJrMoe0COO8M3GQ5OxwqifhdJyVxEElNo-HCdKDSi_OWxRIqV0q8RXoSuIdZ04PAXhRQe4rCz3A0FrYQh1qgRWePC8q36eyb9x7KtUPWS9o3uVSRGW1vrf1_d4lq9I8JV_9_Nf5dF0oU113zmbLSDmEkhgbodXEmZPHhGvlmjHZAVUFWGV4RVr2auSPsDWEb89ZGBkhr9fNwW59GcDC--xBQ2yCy-G-s6WWun5_qYcEAFahDe3mfxrG1jQUKh4Ngz-rVVaiuQiDYCvOlvMiej-RUJqp-OpHNcljUmDxBq64vZq9gxDIX9ApdADPoyLwdL-2_oc1iSSKazm-4eJPT5Gr998zO6F26tphmsO8/4e1/nPwUX3yGTFm5-WfOaJRSgw/h21/h001.LKG1NXTj49Ao7jY4F-kuV5jV9QimtqL-TnHLavazUPA), largely among children. The case count includes 13 hospitalizations.

The vast majority of the cases are from residents of Gaines County about 350 miles west of Dallas, where the outbreak appears to have originated. But there are now additional cases identified in the nearby counties of Lynn, Terry and Yoakum.

**Lara Anton** , a spokesperson for the state health department, said the outbreak appears to have originated in a Mennonite community in Gaines County. The Mennonite church is not widely opposed to vaccination. Mennonite leaders have talked publicly about their COVID vaccinations, for instance, and numerous leaders did not support religious exemptions for COVID vaccines.

All of the confirmed cases are among people who are either unvaccinated or whose vaccination status was unknown, according to the state health department.

Measles is so contagious that during an outbreak “it’s very hard to keep it from spreading in a community that has low vaccination rates,” Anton said.

The case count now surpasses the number of cases seen in a 2013 outbreak. In that outbreak, a person returned from a trip to Asia with the disease, and the cases spread [through a Tarrant County megachurch](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eOQPXJLmqZGCIlkWp25_d_k8skKWbznTburfwsbjEDE6wBrB2_WPkx7o8FBSQfq6T06oaUvqHoDCma18zlHa6NVMlYfwwwVi5TxZhCGbOH0dRuE_RXKC2S590YJyOVyyROzZPJrw5bEDYR32rHvBUYQNMX2kJX_k0CAyThlUt5JMVz_3eENzxslsi6BLGkGsPTJxM8N-IjYphulqLDaNUApEmRxMyiI1hKusZV4ebGX2lKu_glF6Rj0zySdnut3JPNFXoy3dmbUkRI_u1X2X2OZFLFq9osbImh6swkRQVdyU8b8SgvAkHMjLaXbcHNsAHyLHEwAc4eIKFo3SKZ4yWvqVUdY-xA774xMTOa8XXIZn8pbFw_xt1v9OJgr0rSTs1hFlPUPNqBOAtBy7GCd3IxwJMBy7tBKNag5WGJuwgbgXipj9qaiyP3w-C5A548GQHlAva_G8zvDSGs-dHGIigPD59M_j7G4YG9akhtTrkeZRVsWZ_peCz6btPWo9ipREXHicVXZKGA_wp8fNbnJPbBR3hYQKljtgQX9FC9-fbLAt/4e1/nPwUX3yGTFm5-WfOaJRSgw/h23/h001.49UrRNaV2j1MUCKh8JXuoVPnO-nArCLvu8ufCNNKQEI). The South Plains outbreak already has a higher case count than the state saw in all of 2013. Anton said the case count in the ongoing outbreak may still increase as more cases are identified in the coming days and weeks.

Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr . | TOP NEWS “Measles outbreak is Texas ' largest in 30 years,” Dallas Morning News'Emily Brindley — “The ongoing measles outbreak in Texas’ South Plains region is now the largest the state has seen in more than three decades. The Texas Department of State Health Services reported on Friday there are now , largely among children. The case count includes 13 hospitalizations. The vast majority of the cases are from residents of Gaines County about 350 miles west of Dallas, where the outbreak appears to have originated. But there are now additional cases identified in the nearby counties of Lynn, Terry and Yoakum. Lara Anton , a spokesperson for the state health department, said the outbreak appears to have originated in a Mennonite community in Gaines County. The Mennonite church is not widely opposed to vaccination. Mennonite leaders have talked publicly about their COVID vaccinations, for instance, and numerous leaders did not support religious exemptions for COVID vaccines. » All of the confirmed cases are among people who are either unvaccinated or whose vaccination status was unknown, according to the state health department. Measles is so contagious that during an outbreak “it’s very hard to keep it from spreading in a community that has low vaccination rates,” Anton said. The case count now surpasses the number of cases seen in a 2013 outbreak. In that outbreak, a person returned from a trip to Asia with the disease, and the cases spread . In total, Texas saw 27 measles cases in 2013, which was the highest yearly count in the state in two decades. The South Plains outbreak already has a higher case count than the state saw in all of 2013. Anton said the case count in the ongoing outbreak may still increase as more cases are identified in the coming days and weeks. » The state health department is urging Texans to get their measles vaccination if they have not already. The two-dose vaccine regimen offers about against the highly contagious virus. People who are not vaccinated are highly likely to contract the virus if they are exposed to it. Measles is one of the most contagious viruses. The virus can live in the air and on surfaces for after an infected person has left — meaning that unvaccinated people in particular can catch the illness even without being in the same room as an infected person." ---
Peter, I'll cut straight to the point.

**We are a month and a half away from _TWO_ Florida congressional seats being up for special election, and this is the current state of our Majority:**

[
](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=48683f90ccd344b987cc876afec69526&_e=nWl90X5sYcUrmfOnhd3LpifPSw53MDWwtIo_GDYyToql1oqGtbu3LSkibGuuQ9kyyWX0AfwaRKco_oYIKKHrYhbh8YQ6BVauiUA9uq_5t0mBqJlC4IkPbXeydyhl5wUTnFCwD9NamxY32kkdWgzapOiURP62sHym-qnPqt7xphp2PNSE7pdwXhx_MwoCmeStoq4IggQEj2VMBxdXI4A3AxD9dCULuaVrL0aR9i2qPqU0FNGQisbhcoTqiOhwvl7w8Q5NGax2nocpplL4xpk-GIqAqos7-kn6xlq6pNyl4etgi4mkce_WToUlsODIQiMBVKkwJmeUZsVPPyF0ISpnc9Tgz7k_nb3CnyPdPRFurz3BopqUOXfdj2tGHUcXznAB8lydO2VLGQXNhSvuovMG6w%3D%3D)

name: Hillsdale College via American Action News
subject: Take our Critical Race Theory Survey
body:
There’s a tough question that I have to ask you on this fine day.There’s a tough question that I have to ask you on this fine day.

This sponsored message from American Action News is on behalf of Hillsdale College
| | | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zaaqr30pc6ljo1g3eu4ri1c6h96og1btq5b2srdo_rp22sh2s8i6epj4sdi6eoj6cjh2bs)
---
| | Dear Fellow American,

I'm glad you opened my email today! I have something urgent to ask you that will shape the future of our families, communities, and country.

[**Will you take our National Opinion Survey on Critical Race Theory (CRT)?**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zus0rm3q3akgv0cs6hjpt27dctrpe1qatusccir8_rp22sh2s8i6epj4sdi6eoj6cjh2bs)

Your participation will help us understand and address many Americans' concerns about CRT's influence on our society.

This survey will only take just a few minutes.

But in those few minutes, you'll help us gather important insights to defend America's founding principles of liberty...

...and ensure a bright future for our children and grandchildren.

**You can share your thoughts on CRT in the National Opinion Survey on Critical Race Theory Survey here:**

[**https://info.hillsdale.edu/crt-survey/**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z5q7ki7dvlfmhld1s6oekt4bqnrlslacfrj7doq8_rp22sh2s8i6epj4sdi6eoj6cjh2bs)

Sharing your opinion is an important way you'll combat the spread of CRT in our schools and workplaces. Together, we can preserve the constitutional and free-enterprise principles that made our nation so great and so free.

Thanks in advance! I look forward to seeing your response.

Bill Gray, '01
Vice President
Institutional Advancement
Hillsdale College
---
| |
---

American Action News
---
_PO Box 1177 Fort Collins, CO 80522_

name: Hillsdale College via Updates For Great Americans
subject: Take our Critical Race Theory Survey
body:
There’s a tough question that I have to ask you on this fine day.There’s a tough question that I have to ask you on this fine day.

This sponsored message from Great America Update is on behalf of Hillsdale College
| | | [](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z118nrlburl2ipig10sg5bbg10shfudp5r7ig11o_rp22sh2s8i6epj4sdi69o32cjh2bs)
---
| | Dear Fellow American,

I'm glad you opened my email today! I have something urgent to ask you that will shape the future of our families, communities, and country.

[**Will you take our National Opinion Survey on Critical Race Theory (CRT)?**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2znv420dc6hii385rbt3ntqn0qrts29thdko0af2g_rp22sh2s8i6epj4sdi69o32cjh2bs)

Your participation will help us understand and address many Americans' concerns about CRT's influence on our society.

This survey will only take just a few minutes.

But in those few minutes, you'll help us gather important insights to defend America's founding principles of liberty...

...and ensure a bright future for our children and grandchildren.

**You can share your thoughts on CRT in the National Opinion Survey on Critical Race Theory Survey here:**

[**https://info.hillsdale.edu/crt-survey/**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zq4frnua0mc53gb3m4n0c1f77qju6lm2hbi4pmk8_rp22sh2s8i6epj4sdi69o32cjh2bs)

Sharing your opinion is an important way you'll combat the spread of CRT in our schools and workplaces. Together, we can preserve the constitutional and free-enterprise principles that made our nation so great and so free.

Thanks in advance! I look forward to seeing your response.

Bill Gray, '01
Vice President
Institutional Advancement
Hillsdale College
---
| |
---

Updates for Great Americans
---
_PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522_
_[**unsubscribe**](http://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z2jt3hfi1a78q1rq2cgj6drm21t1dujmm8nbkhv8_rp2ash2s8gb2sbgebjqf9tge5rrasbjklo72d1hekr76dtiesp7ad1hegqracjkmoq37djjkorrbdt5eer7ashmeop79chl6ujuadf161rn9cdhmir76pr02sqr8d5hmej3fddj4iqu8ob0chi64o7246h22sh2s8i6epj4sdi69o32cjh2bs)_ | _[manage data preferences](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z5ipr8st96mp85fc8rhehk23dlmn48ondtmb0pk0_rp22sh2s8i6epj4sdi69o32cjh2bs)_

**[BIG NEWS:](https://e.realamericanreporting.com/rd/9z2z2i7v1o1a40l8fpqee22525nu844454ct66nc9b0_rp22sh2s8i6epj4sdi69o32cjh2bs) **Republicans now have an 8-POINT LEAD over Democrats for control of the House and Senate.

name: Jim Jordan
subject: Impeachment! Here’s what happens next:
body:
Imagine this scene of heartbreak!

SHUT DOWN the Democrats’ new impeachment hoax.
| [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z9v2tta11vl14oooddfe5421vjo20j0o2vv5rvpo_rp22sh2s8i6epj4sdh60o76cph2bs)
---
Friend,
---
They truly never learn...
---
**“Donald Trump Impeachment Articles Filed. Here's What Happens Next”** [Source: _Newsweek_]
---
Voters like YOU made your voices heard loud and clear last fall when you sent President Trump back to the White House with Republican majorities in Congress.
---
But the Democrats couldn’t care less about the will of the _PEOPLE_.
---
In fact, the Radical Democrat who filed these articles even claimed that impeachment doesn’t require a crime at all. He simply said, “the word _misdemeanor_ means misdeeds.”
---
In other words, the Democrats believe they can **IMPEACH** the duly elected President of the United States so long as they don’t like him.
---
_That’s how little the Left respects our Republic._
---
Friend, make no mistake, there’s only ONE thing that should _“happen next:”_ **These sham articles of impeachment should be thrown to the ash heap!**
---
_Which is why I’m reaching out to every grassroots patriot I can to ask that you sign your name to demand the Democrats' latest sham impeachment hoax be SHUT DOWN IMMEDIATELY._
---
[**Please add your name right away to stand with President Trump and DEMAND the Democrats’ new impeachment hoax be thrown to the ash heap.**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z2911r120rqibb5aoiuf8negr5eb9ngqf6sohlt8_rp22sh2s8i6epj4sdh60o76cph2bs)

name: Ryan Zinke, Navy SEAL
subject: Dead in the water
disclaimer_text: Paid for by ZINKE FOR CONGRESS
body:
It could be over before it even starts.

| |
---
| | | I'm running out of time.

The media labeled my district a serious **battleground** race, naming _**ME**_ one of America's most vulnerable Republicans right now.

The Democrats have unleashed a fundraising juggernaut in Montana before, and now, they just dumped $50 MILLION into House races across America to destroy conservatives. _**[My campaign could be over before it even starts, and our conservative reforms could be dead in the water if I don't raise the necessary funds in the next 48 hours.](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zgv665eaveqlho41vhs3d8peghb6icdr4m1fluig_rp22sh2s8i6epj4sdh60o76chh2bs "My campaign could be over before it even starts, and our conservative reforms could be dead in the water if I don't raise the necessary funds in the next 48 hours.")**_
---
| | [DONATE NOW >>](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2z99cjnqpu4biqtpgvq6262tfef6j3ocbgqa57sio_rp22sh2s8i6epj4sdh60o76chh2bs)
---
It's not just my seat that's on the line. If the Democrats can take me down, they'll crush any hope of Republicans holding the majority in Congress. With only a 3-seat Majority, conservatives' odds of winning are quite slim.

As a Navy SEAL and Cabinet member for President Trump, I've fought for this nation my entire life. But I've never faced a challenge like this. **Democrats are on the warpath to get revenge after their embarrassing loss last year, and liberal elites are willing to open their checkbooks to make it happen. **

I only have 48 hours to do a complete 180 and keep up with their war chest. That's why I'm turning to you. I need your help to blow my fundraising goal out of the water and stop the Democrats' plan to destroy our nation and our values.

**_[If you donate right now- right this minute, we WILL crush our fundraising goals and stop the Democrats' revenge tour from destroying our great nation.](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zi7fphutcd59js9c0logf9cmntk87uuan3qo9p70_rp22sh2s8i6epj4sdh60o76chh2bs "If you donate right now – right this minute, we WILL crush our fundraising goals and stop the Democrats’ revenge tour from destroying our great nation.")_ **
---
| | [DONATE NOW >>](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2z8i09mamhqrtgq4cqb6i9aidu1ro082b6mat8gf8_rp22sh2s8i6epj4sdh60o76chh2bs)
---
I've led teams of Navy SEALs into battle, I've led a team of Navy SEALs into Congress, and in both, I fought against impossible odds. But I've never given up. And I won't give up on you now.

Thank you for your support,
Ryan Zinke

---

name: The Liberty Ledger
subject: Border czar Tom Homan says AOC could be in trouble for advising illegal aliens how to deal with ICE, calls for investigation
body:
Border czar Tom Homan hinted that Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) interfered with deportation operations by giving advice to illegal immigrants on how to deal with agents of Immigration and Customs Enforcement. On Wednesday, AOC's office hosted a live hour-long webinar titled "Know Your Rights" on the representative's official government Facebook social media page. The webinar provided illegal aliens with tactics on how to deal with ICE agents looking to deport them. 'Impediment is impediment, in my opinion. | [](http://trk.libertyledger.-VqUMoF7ow)
