Dear Peter, Has your monthly energy bill gone up in recent years? Peter, utility rate hikes in Oregon have gone too far. We need your voice this legislative session to ensure lawmakers invest in energy efficiency and affordability. In the 2025 legislative session, lawmakers have two choices: Make long overdue investments in programs to help keep energy costs down and upgrade housing to be more weather resilient, or 2. Slash the already scarce funding for existing programs. **We must ensure Oregon continues to build on our progress toward a clean energy future that protects our families and communities from extreme weather and climate disasters - and protects ratepayers from higher costs.**
Have you made energy upgrades to your home, or are you planning to? Do you need help finding resources to navigate increasingly extreme hot and cold weather? Have you been impacted by rate hikes? Lawmakers need to hear your story. [**Take our brief survey**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/102196870/528396721/1723080746?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjI0ZGQxMzE3LWNmZWItZWYxMS05MGNiLTAwMjI0ODJhOTRmNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=wLzqB8Yu-GNP5Ex_2jlCQxpHM8qHwlAmp5PVKRFS3fM=&emci=f50ebf95-06eb-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=24dd1317-cfeb-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=2107230)**to get connected with opportunities to push the State of Oregon to address our utility rate and energy efficiency crisis.**
---
[**Take our survey**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/102196871/528396722/1723080746?contactdata=faKWbSU3qgSvH22ydGEUcHMtZi5sQICSR/CVDnYLE5r1ZxjJIR1a7vEIMpkQH+I6bCIziIHkOu1UzXpsm79M55IRwmek/5gd/b4PNfZ/F1e8Ne9XKIJCcRmEZ09XXnt2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjI0ZGQxMzE3LWNmZWItZWYxMS05MGNiLTAwMjI0ODJhOTRmNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=wLzqB8Yu-GNP5Ex_2jlCQxpHM8qHwlAmp5PVKRFS3fM=&emci=f50ebf95-06eb-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=24dd1317-cfeb-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=2107230)

It will help us connect you with opportunities big and small — from signing a petition, to educating yourself and your family about existing programs which you could take advantage of, to writing a letter to the editor or even going to Salem and telling your story to lawmakers. Thank you for taking this first, small step with us. clean_body: I hope you are all staying warm with this winter weather! This storm has got me thinking...| | | Hi Peter, I hope you all enjoyed the snow and have been staying warm! With this winter weather, I’ve been thinking a lot about my energy bills — and I’m sure you have too. In the last 5 months since moving to Portland, I have noticed a drastic increase in my energy bill. PGE’s rates are significantly higher than the energy bills I had in Eugene with EWEB. I’m someone who’s careful about my energy use, but with this winter storm weather, I’ve had to turn my heat up. My rental apartment still has outdated baseboard heaters, as do many other homes, which makes for super inefficient heating. It’s weather like this and outdated inefficient systems, like baseboard heaters, that drive up energy prices a lot. | | That is just my story, but I know thousands of Oregonians like myself are struggling with rising utility and energy bills. I’d like to hear from you; what’s your story? | | | Stay warm, Laurel Viles (she/her) OLCV Engagement Organizer | Dear Peter, Has your monthly energy bill gone up in recent years? Peter, utility rate hikes in Oregon have gone too far. We need your voice this legislative session to ensure lawmakers invest in energy efficiency and affordability. In the 2025 legislative session, lawmakers have two choices: Make long overdue investments in programs to help keep energy costs down and upgrade housing to be more weather resilient, or 2. Slash the already scarce funding for existing programs. We must ensure Oregon continues to build on our progress toward a clean energy future that protects our families and communities from extreme weather and climate disasters - and protects ratepayers from higher costs. Have you made energy upgrades to your home, or are you planning to? Do you need help finding resources to navigate increasingly extreme hot and cold weather? Have you been impacted by rate hikes? Lawmakers need to hear your story. get connected with opportunities to push the State of Oregon to address our utility rate and energy efficiency crisis. | | This survey takes less than 90 seconds to complete but will make a tremendous impact on our state's energy future. It will help us connect you with opportunities big and small — from signing a petition, to educating yourself and your family about existing programs which you could take advantage of, to writing a letter to the editor or even going to Salem and telling your story to lawmakers. Thank you for taking this first, small step with us. share real stories from every Oregonians and show lawmakers that the time to act is now. Feel free to share this survey with your friends! Sincerely, Britney VanCitters (she/her) OLCV Political & Organizing Director | | | | | ---|---|--- Oregon League of Conservation Voters 1110 SE Alder Suite 220 Portland, OR 97214 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please .

body:
Let’s keep the momentum going.

| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/102197887/528399614/1328794144?refcode=ea.b41ed1cc-d0eb-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ms=ea.b41ed1cc-d0eb-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImI0MWVkMWNjLWQwZWItZWYxMS05MGNiLTAwMjI0ODJhOTRmNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=WLWHRwXgFBBCBy4UKY6NfS-LQIaZYjmHEKl28qBv1uM=&emci=174016c5-d0eb-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=b41ed1cc-d0eb-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=4823105)
---
Last month, we told you about our endorsed candidate Kannan Srinivasan’s big win in Virginia — a critical special election that secured a Democratic majority in the legislature. We backed Kannan because we knew what was at stake. Well, here’s the impact of that victory: **In just the last three weeks, Virginia Democrats have used their new majority to pass constitutional amendments protecting abortion access, voting rights, and marriage equality.** [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/102197888/528399615/2105508926?refcode=ea.b41ed1cc-d0eb-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ms=ea.b41ed1cc-d0eb-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImI0MWVkMWNjLWQwZWItZWYxMS05MGNiLTAwMjI0ODJhOTRmNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=WLWHRwXgFBBCBy4UKY6NfS-LQIaZYjmHEKl28qBv1uM=) | [**CHIP IN TO FUEL MORE WINS LIKE THIS**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/102197889/528399616/2105508926?refcode=ea.b41ed1cc-d0eb-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ms=ea.b41ed1cc-d0eb-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImI0MWVkMWNjLWQwZWItZWYxMS05MGNiLTAwMjI0ODJhOTRmNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=WLWHRwXgFBBCBy4UKY6NfS-LQIaZYjmHEKl28qBv1uM=)
---
Think about that: One race secured a majority and delivered life-changing protections for millions of Virginians.
This is why elections matter. This is why every race matters.
While chaos unfolds in Washington, D.C., we’re building firewalls in state legislatures to defend our rights, protect the climate, and fight back against Trump’s radical agenda.
But we can’t stop here. Trump and his Republican allies are already plotting their next moves, and we need to be ready to meet them head-on.
**[Can you chip in today to help us fuel more victories like this? Every dollar powers campaigns that hold the line where it matters most.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/102197890/528399617/2105508926?refcode=ea.b41ed1cc-d0eb-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ms=ea.b41ed1cc-d0eb-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImI0MWVkMWNjLWQwZWItZWYxMS05MGNiLTAwMjI0ODJhOTRmNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=WLWHRwXgFBBCBy4UKY6NfS-LQIaZYjmHEKl28qBv1uM=)**

clean_body: Let’s keep the momentum going. | Last month, we told you about our endorsed candidate Kannan Srinivasan’s big win in Virginia — a critical special election that secured a Democratic majority in the legislature. We backed Kannan because we knew what was at stake. Well, here’s the impact of that victory: In just the last three weeks, Virginia Democrats have used their new majority to pass constitutional amendments protecting abortion access, voting rights, and marriage equality. = | = Think about that: One race secured a majority and delivered life-changing protections for millions of Virginians. This is why elections matter. This is why every race matters. While chaos unfolds in Washington, D.C., we’re building firewalls in state legislatures to defend our rights, protect the climate, and fight back against Trump’s radical agenda. But we can’t stop here. Trump and his Republican allies are already plotting their next moves, and we need to be ready to meet them head-on. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Together, we’re proving that grassroots power wins. Let’s keep going. Team JanePAC | = | = = Follow along: | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. Jane Fonda Climate PAC 600 Pennsylvania Ave SE #15180 Washington, DC 20003 United States

body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/9?t=1&akid=39440%2E3422442%2Eeb1GBm)
Derek, we wanted to share some of the work your donation to Truth to Power supports.
But first, our mid-month deadline is just hours away. If you’re ready to chip in, use this custom link we made for you: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ttp-em-20250215?personal=dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ttp-em-20250215?refcode=em20250215-39440&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39440_3422442_eb1GBm&akid=39440%2E3422442%2Eeb1GBm)
A contribution could help support majority-makers like **Derek Tran** (CA-45), who won his race by _just 653 votes_ last year.
It provides valuable resources to trailblazers like **Sarah McBride** of Delaware, who is the first openly transgender person to serve as a Member of Congress.
Or it could go towards finding the **next class of bold change-makers** we will send to Congress to stand up to Donald Trump.
Our mid-month fundraising deadline is at midnight and we’re just short of our $45,000 goal. Join our fight by chipping in $5 today at this link we made just for you: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ttp-em-20250215?personal=dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ttp-em-20250215?refcode=em20250215-39440&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39440_3422442_eb1GBm&akid=39440%2E3422442%2Eeb1GBm)
Thank you,
Team Truth to Power

Former U.S. Congresswoman Katie Porter founded Truth to Power to support the campaigns of her fellow Democrats. Truth to Power supports candidates who will stand up to Donald Trump, fight to lower costs, and protect the environment. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/truth-to-power-1?refcode=em20250215-39440&amounts=&t=2&refcode2=39440_3422442_eb1GBm&akid=39440%2E3422442%2Eeb1GBm)**

name: Janelle Bynum
subject: What my parents taught me
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JANELLE BYNUM FOR CONGRESS
body:
My parents taught me a lot of things over the years: Treat others the way you want to be treated; Learn from your mistakes; Never give up; Etc. However, there’s one lesson that has come to stand above the rest. A lesson that’s guided my entire time in public office. Education is key to a better, safer world.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102199237/528403659/476240477?amount=10&refcode=em_fr_2025.02.15_b1_jb_all&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDY3MjEiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOGZhMjMwMzctZDZlYi1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5NGY0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=_LcZQ79fTjRJOu3vF7Py_0QVYnoFWMwCAxQNFSnIHF0=)
My parents taught me a lot of things over the years: Treat others the way you want to be treated; Learn from your mistakes; Never give up; Etc.

However, there’s one lesson that has come to stand above the rest. A lesson that’s guided my entire time in public office. **Education is key to a better, safer world.**
**[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102199238/528403660/476240477?amount=10&refcode=em_fr_2025.02.15_b1_jb_all&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDY3MjEiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOGZhMjMwMzctZDZlYi1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5NGY0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=_LcZQ79fTjRJOu3vF7Py_0QVYnoFWMwCAxQNFSnIHF0=)**_My fifth-grade school picture._
My parents, who were both teachers, instilled in me the importance of education from a very young age. I spent my afternoons reading and my weekends attending engineering classes designed to help minority students pursue careers in engineering.

And with that, I was able to earn scholarships and put myself through school, earning a Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering and a Master’s degree in Business Administration.

I know, firsthand, the importance of educational opportunity. **But these days, access to quality, affordable education feels out of reach for so many families across the country.**

The cost of higher education is skyrocketing. Student loan debt is out of control. Teachers and students don’t have the resources they need to succeed inside and outside the classroom. **If educational opportunity is essential to creating a better America, our leaders need to act like it.**

So that’s why I ran for Congress in 2023... and won. I’m so proud to be putting my parents’ teachings into practice and to be fighting for our kids in Congress. We have a lot of work to do, but I’ll never stop working hard until the hard work is done.

[**If you agree that children across Oregon deserve leaders who will fight for their access to quality and affordable education, I hope you’ll stand with me and make a donation to my re-election campaign today.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102199239/528403661/476240477?amount=10&refcode=em_fr_2025.02.15_b1_jb_all&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDY3MjEiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOGZhMjMwMzctZDZlYi1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5NGY0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=_LcZQ79fTjRJOu3vF7Py_0QVYnoFWMwCAxQNFSnIHF0=)
| [**DONATE**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102199240/528403662/476240477?amount=10&refcode=em_fr_2025.02.15_b1_jb_all&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDY3MjEiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOGZhMjMwMzctZDZlYi1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5NGY0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=_LcZQ79fTjRJOu3vF7Py_0QVYnoFWMwCAxQNFSnIHF0=)

Onward,

_Janelle_

**Janelle Bynum represents Oregon's Fifth Congressional District. With a reputation as a tireless champion for real progress, Janelle has worked in the State Legislature to safeguard our rights, lower costs, and create pathways to success for the next generation.

Oregon's 5th District is one of the most competitive seats in the country. **First** , Donald Trump was elected president.

clean_body: | | | Trump officials promote eugenics The Trump administration has repeatedly shown its true colors, with alarming instances of white supremacy and eugenics ideology. A recent revelation has come to light: a State Department official appointed by Trump advocated for the mass sterilization of what he called "low-IQ trash." He said, “Higher quality humans are subsidizing the fertility of lower quality humans.” This is not an isolated incident but part of a troubling pattern among MAGA that threatens the fabric of our democracy and the values we hold dear. To combat this dangerous rhetoric and defeat Trump and his MAGA minions, Able Dems runs ads and billboards against vulnerable Republicans. Our goal is to raise awareness and rally citizens against these divisive ideologies. We need your help to make this campaign a success. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. Donald Trump has openly mocked a disabled reporter. name: Eric Swalwell
subject: Don’t let them distract you, friend.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
This is all their design. But we won’t take our eyes off the ball.
| We knew that Trump would use his first month in office to unleash his extreme MAGA agenda on Americans. And friend, that’s exactly what he and his cronies have done from day one.

Trump and his allies are throwing so much at us all at once in hopes they can beat us down, sneak dangerous policies into law, and strengthen their full-fledged bro-ligargy — **_and that’s exactly why we can’t take our eyes off the ball._**

[Friend, with the onslaught of attacks on our rights, I’m working with Democrats like the folks at Voter Protection Project to make sure you’re alert, informed, and ready to join us in the fight to protect our freedoms at a moment’s notice. Can you split a $10 donation between my campaign and Voter Protection Project to support our fight? »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/86942?t=6&akid=18941%2E878179%2EYsZxSL) If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Eric Swalwell and VPP will go through immediately: | [CONTRIBUTE $10 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/86943?t=7&akid=18941%2E878179%2EYsZxSL)
---
[CONTRIBUTE $25 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/86944?t=8&akid=18941%2E878179%2EYsZxSL)
[CONTRIBUTE $50 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/86945?t=9&akid=18941%2E878179%2EYsZxSL)
[CONTRIBUTE $100 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/86946?t=10&akid=18941%2E878179%2EYsZxSL)
[Or a custom donation »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/86947?t=11&akid=18941%2E878179%2EYsZxSL)
It’s easy to focus on the scary topical stuff: Elon proudly giving the Nazi salute, Trump’s team scrubbing key offices from the White House website, and more is all a show of MAGA’s values (or lack thereof). _But friend, those are designed to distract us._

**Trump and his team are working behind the scenes every day to eradicate reproductive rights, end prescription drug price caps, and make life hell for Americans like us. That’s why we need to stand together, keep each other alert and engaged, and keep holding him accountable.**

[Are you with us? Split a contribution to my campaign and Voter Protection Project so we can continue fighting on the frontlines against the MAGA mob. »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/86942?t=12&akid=18941%2E878179%2EYsZxSL)
[DONATE TODAY »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/86942?t=13&akid=18941%2E878179%2EYsZxSL)
---
Friend, we’ll have to take this administration day by day, and goal by goal — but it starts with you.

Thanks for being in this fight,
Eric Swalwell
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=18941%2E878179%2EYsZxSL). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=18941%2E878179%2EYsZxSL).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=18941%2E878179%2EYsZxSL) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=14&akid=18941%2E878179%2EYsZxSL). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=18941%2E878179%2EYsZxSL)
---
[Donate Just One Time »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/124?t=5&akid=18941%2E878179%2EYsZxSL)

name: Jim Jordan
subject: Impeachment! Here’s what happens next:
body:
SHUT DOWN the Democrats’ new impeachment hoax.

SHUT DOWN the Democrats’ new impeachment hoax.
| [](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/a1q8/?__vbtrk=Mjc1Njc5OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjc1Njc5OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
---
Friend,
---
They truly never learn...
---
**“Donald Trump Impeachment Articles Filed. Here's What Happens Next”** [Source: _Newsweek_]
---
Voters like YOU made your voices heard loud and clear last fall when you sent President Trump back to the White House with Republican majorities in Congress.
---
But the Democrats couldn’t care less about the will of the _PEOPLE_.
---
In fact, the Radical Democrat who filed these articles even claimed that impeachment doesn’t require a crime at all. He simply said, “the word _misdemeanor_ means misdeeds.”
---
In other words, the Democrats believe they can **IMPEACH** the duly elected President of the United States so long as they don’t like him.
---
_That’s how little the Left respects our Republic._
---
Friend, make no mistake, there’s only ONE thing that should _“happen next:”_ **These sham articles of impeachment should be thrown to the ash heap!**
---
_Which is why I’m reaching out to every grassroots patriot I can to ask that you sign your name to demand the Democrats' latest sham impeachment hoax be SHUT DOWN IMMEDIATELY._
---
[**Please add your name right away to stand with President Trump and DEMAND the Democrats’ new impeachment hoax be thrown to the ash heap.**](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/a1q8/?__vbtrk=Mjc1Njc5OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjc1Njc5OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)

---

Thank you,

body:
This is not an "off-year" election. It’s a pivotal moment for Virginia — and for the entire country, especially as we continue to see chaos and division out of Washington.

| Peter,

This is not an "off-year" election. It’s a pivotal moment for Virginia — and for the entire country, especially as we continue to see chaos and division out of Washington.

[_**We’ll explain more below, but first, can we count on your contribution to Abigail’s campaign for Governor to keep us on track for the month?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102201218/528407701/1993432096?refcode=BLU_EM_ASFG_2025-02-15_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMGFmZmViNzktZDhlYi1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5NGY0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=vsC6y8YMVJei7CCBSGiHEEVVL8c2nbM_5FhPywhLyvE=)

---
It’s been less than a month into Trump’s presidency, and we’re already seeing attacks on our democracy, our rights, and our communities while Virginia’s Republican leadership stands by his side and supports him with glee.

Virginia deserves a Governor who is ready to stand up to the chaos and deliver results for our Commonwealth.

Abigail has spent her life serving our country and the Commonwealth, from her time as a federal law enforcement officer to her undercover work with the CIA. She’s always put the safety and well-being of Americans first—and now, as our next Governor, she will do the same for Virginia.

[_**But the polls show that this race is going to be incredibly close, so we need your help! Can you contribute just $3 today to make sure we reach every voter across Virginia and flip this seat blue in November?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102201220/528407703/1993432096?refcode=BLU_EM_ASFG_2025-02-15_1&amount=3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMGFmZmViNzktZDhlYi1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5NGY0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=vsC6y8YMVJei7CCBSGiHEEVVL8c2nbM_5FhPywhLyvE=)

clean_body: This is not an "off-year" election. It’s a pivotal moment for Virginia — and for the entire country, especially as we continue to see chaos and division out of Washington. | = | Peter, This is not an "off-year" election. It’s a pivotal moment for Virginia — and for the entire country, especially as we continue to see chaos and division out of Washington. = | = It’s been less than a month into Trump’s presidency, and we’re already seeing attacks on our democracy, our rights, and our communities while Virginia’s Republican leadership stands by his side and supports him with glee. Virginia deserves a Governor who is ready to stand up to the chaos and deliver results for our Commonwealth. Abigail has spent her life serving our country and the Commonwealth, from her time as a federal law enforcement officer to her undercover work with the CIA. She’s always put the safety and well-being of Americans first—and now, as our next Governor, she will do the same for Virginia. = | | = | = | = | | = | = | = | = Thank you, Team Spanberger * | | Paid for by Spanberger for Governor Spanberger for Governor P.O. Peter, Donald Trump and his allies in Congress have had full control of the government for a little less than a month, and they’ve already unleashed a wave of chaos unlike anything we’ve seen before. Trump has issued multiple executive orders that have violated the Constitution — orders that make it easier for billionaires and large corporations to line their pockets at the expense of working families and do nothing to meaningfully address the high costs of groceries, housing, and child care. Unelected billionaire Elon Musk is dismantling critical federal agencies that everyday Americans rely on, shutting down public services, and fighting to gain control of your personal information. And House Republicans are working around the clock to pass a massive tax cut for billionaires that’s paid for by your hard-earned benefits. This isn’t an exaggeration, Peter. **Grassroots supporters like you make Tina’s work to move Oregon forward possible.** It’s true! The donations that you make to emails like this one allow us to reach Oregonians in every corner of the state, advocate for our shared values, and help Tina make real progress for working families. As she takes bold action on the issues that impact our state the most, your support fuels her fight to address homelessness, protect reproductive health care, and safeguard our rights against Donald Trump and his allies, who are spending their time attacking the values that make our state great. Tina has big plans for 2025 to continue making real progress on the issues that matter to Oregonians, like improving access to mental health and addiction treatment and increasing funding and accountability in our schools — but she can’t do it alone, Peter. subject: Here’s the issue, friend

body:

# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102202083/528410114/-822722586?refcode=AU_NJB_FR_GEN_MQG_AC120_20250215_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxY2IyMTJhOC1kYmViLWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5I_-LFajqpcYG8_8NQhE6ZjE_XlyLTTVFi5AHOVLXa0=)

Friend, I’ll cut straight to the chase: We’ve seen a slight dip in our grassroots fundraising lately, and we’re at risk of falling behind a critical goal. Considering many folks are burnt out from endless headlines about Donald Trump’s harmful executive actions, this isn’t surprising. **But here’s the issue:** Republicans know that the path to defending their narrow House majority requires flipping seats like mine next year, so they’re counting on folks being too tired or distracted to engage with the races that matter in 2026. **And here’s the good news:** Our grassroots team is no stranger to tough fights. We’ve overcome obstacles together in the past and ran a strong campaign all the way through November last year. Thousands of grassroots supporters pitched in to help power us over the finish line, and I know we can do it again if we work together now to prepare for the obstacles we’ll face down the road. **[So friend, before our first mid-quarter deadline of the year expires tonight at midnight, can we count on you to chip in $5, $10, or whatever you can to help get us back on track so I can continue my work for IL-13 and our country?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102202084/528410115/-1697794532?amount=&refcode=AU_NJB_FR_GEN_MQG_AC120_20250215_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxY2IyMTJhOC1kYmViLWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5I_-LFajqpcYG8_8NQhE6ZjE_XlyLTTVFi5AHOVLXa0=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [PITCH IN $5 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102202085/528410116/-1697794532?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=AU_NJB_FR_GEN_MQG_AC120_20250215_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxY2IyMTJhOC1kYmViLWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5I_-LFajqpcYG8_8NQhE6ZjE_XlyLTTVFi5AHOVLXa0=)

> Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr [here](https://substack.com/redirect/98319209-727b-49ac-9ee2-df46cdacc285?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* * *
* **TOP NEWS**

"**Measles outbreak is Texas' largest in 30 years,** " Dallas Morning News'**Emily Brindley** -- "The ongoing measles outbreak in Texas' South Plains region is now the largest the state has seen in more than three decades.

The Texas Department of State Health Services reported on Friday there are now [48 identified measles cases](https://substack.com/redirect/ebb86a8b-5b68-4b49-81d3-d720e3afad24?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), largely among children. The case count includes 13 hospitalizations.

The vast majority of the cases are from residents of Gaines County about 350 miles west of Dallas, where the outbreak appears to have originated. But there are now additional cases identified in the nearby counties of Lynn, Terry and Yoakum.

**Lara Anton** , a spokesperson for the state health department, said the outbreak appears to have originated in a Mennonite community in Gaines County. The Mennonite church is not widely opposed to vaccination. Mennonite leaders have talked publicly about their COVID vaccinations, for instance, and numerous leaders did not support religious exemptions for COVID vaccines.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/0ee6b962-b866-4c1a-86db-e71e58f22375?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/0ee6b962-b866-4c1a-86db-e71e58f22375?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
All of the confirmed cases are among people who are either unvaccinated or whose vaccination status was unknown, according to the state health department.

Measles is so contagious that during an outbreak "it's very hard to keep it from spreading in a community that has low vaccination rates," Anton said.

The case count now surpasses the number of cases seen in a 2013 outbreak. In that outbreak, a person returned from a trip to Asia with the disease, and the cases spread [through a Tarrant County megachurch](https://substack.com/redirect/a49d37f5-9bb7-4a77-ad3c-4cb28afdbc53?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). In total, Texas saw 27 measles cases in 2013, which was the highest yearly count in the state in two decades.

The South Plains outbreak already has a higher case count than the state saw in all of 2013. Anton said the case count in the ongoing outbreak may still increase as more cases are identified in the coming days and weeks.

The state health department is urging Texans to get their measles vaccination if they have not already.

The two-dose vaccine regimen offers about [97% protection](https://substack.com/redirect/6b14bdbe-4240-46fb-b6bd-3bf10b520251?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) against the highly contagious virus. People who are not vaccinated are highly likely to contract the virus if they are exposed to it.

Measles is one of the most contagious viruses. The virus can live in the air and on surfaces for [up to two hours](https://substack.com/redirect/3544b2f5-967d-4f60-9eab-edf17ec5b72f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) after an infected person has left -- meaning that unvaccinated people in particular can catch the illness even without being in the same room as an infected person." [DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/9d50bef6-7566-495a-b9d0-34fd59bc5af0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

* **#TXLEGE**

"**Texas is poised to become a film haven -- but not without a fight,**" Texas Tribune's**Pooja Salhotra** and **Dan Keemahill** -- "A promise to make Texas the film capital of the world has left local creatives raring to cash in -- and preparing for a legislative battle.

The Texas Senate has proposed injecting $498 million to revamp the state's film incentive program, a historic sum that rivals most other states and more than doubles the $200 million lawmakers plugged into the program during the last budget cycle.

Film industry insiders, who have for years been fighting for a larger and more consistent funding stream, could hardly believe it when they heard the $498 million figure, a line item in the draft budget the Senate filed last month. According to Lt. Gov. **Dan Patrick** , the funding would include $48 million in grants for small films and television commercials and up to $450 million in new tax credits, contingent on a bill passing.

"It's like we are in a 'stars aligned' period," said **Brian Gannon** , director of the Austin Film Commission. "There's alignment across industry, across government. Everyone is excited."" [Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/31c5df3e-ddea-404e-9c11-25d5eccd369d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

* **STATE GOVERNMENT**

"**Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton announces investigation into Chinese AI company DeepSeek,** " Austin American-Statesman's**Karoline Leonard** -- ""Texas Attorney General **Ken Paxton** [announced Friday afternoon](https://substack.com/redirect/e5d565f9-6ca4-44d0-b80c-259568f4bd47?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that he is investigating DeepSeek, a Chinese artificial intelligence company that recently made headlines and caused several American tech stocks to plunge.

According to the announcement, Paxton informed DeepSeek that it violated the Texas Data Privacy and Security Act. The investigation focuses on privacy concerns related to the company's AI platforms and its claims that its AI model rivals OpenAI's. OpenAI, founded by CEO **Sam Altman** and tech billionaire **Elon Musk** in 2015, is known for the popular generative AI platform ChatGPT.

Paxton has issued third-party civil investigative demands to Google and Apple, requesting their analysis of DeepSeek and the documentation that DeepSeek submitted for consumer access.

Paxton's announcement alleges that DeepSeek is "no more than a proxy for the" Chinese Communist Party.

"The United States and Texas will continue to be at the forefront of global AI innovation, and any CCP-aligned company that tries to undermine that dominance by violating the rights of Texans and illegally undercutting American technology companies will face the full force of the law," he said.

Paxton's investigation comes after Texas Gov. **Greg Abbott** banned the use of DeepSeek and other Chinese-backed AI and social media apps on government-issued devices on Jan. 31.

The proclamation directed the Texas Department of Public Safety and the Department of Information Resources to add six technologies -- DeepSeek, Lemon8, Moomoo, RedNote, Tiger Brokers, and Webull -- to the state's prohibited technologies list. This ban prevents state employees and contractors from downloading and using these apps on both personal and state-owned devices.

After banning the short-form video social media app TikTok on government-issued devices back in 2022, Abbott signed Senate Bill 1893, which granted him the authority to ban any social media applications or services that pose potential security risks to Texas." [AAS](https://substack.com/redirect/77183f1a-8d3e-469b-9ccc-2de4355a46fc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

"**Texas Supreme Court dismisses state bar 's lawsuit against Ken Paxton for challenging 2020 presidential election,**" Texas Tribune's**Jasper Scherer** -- "The Texas Supreme Court on Friday dismissed the State Bar of Texas' lawsuit against Attorney General **Ken Paxton** for his attempt to overturn the results of the 2020 presidential election, extending [a cascade of legal and political wins](https://substack.com/redirect/c866fc50-a9cb-465e-9f85-d8142710e1ce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for the once-embattled Republican leader.

The decision came three weeks after lawyers with the bar's Commission for Lawyer Discipline asked for the dismissal, citing the high court's December [decision to toss a separate state bar lawsuit](https://substack.com/redirect/30246813-7e15-4107-8cd0-5870429b428d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) against Paxton's top aide, **Brent Webster** , for working with Paxton to challenge the 2020 outcome in battleground states won by Democrat Joe Biden.

An unsigned opinion from the all-Republican court acknowledged the two cases were "nearly identical" and agreed that the bar's move to voluntarily dismiss the lawsuit "renders this case moot."

The state bar had sought to sanction Paxton, which could have carried a punishment ranging from a private reprimand to disbarment. Lawyers from the bar -- which regulates law licenses in Texas -- have argued that Paxton, in falsely claiming to have uncovered major evidence of election wrongdoing, forced the battleground states "to expend time, money, and resources to respond to the misrepresentations and false statements."

The dismissal ends a case that [dates back to May 2022](https://substack.com/redirect/b0a2c554-4c78-4a14-a721-22aa29007490?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It is Paxton's second personal**** legal victory within the last year: In March, prosecutors [dropped long-running felony securities fraud charges](https://substack.com/redirect/449705e6-ac6b-4637-bdcf-2b757892fa71?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) against Paxton under an agreement that required the attorney general to perform 100 hours of community service and take 15 hours of legal ethics courses. Paxton also agreed to pay around $271,000 in restitution to those he was accused of defrauding more than a decade ago when he allegedly solicited investors in a McKinney technology company without disclosing that the firm was paying him to promote its stock.

Months before the securities fraud case was resolved, Paxton also [survived a series of impeachment charges](https://substack.com/redirect/28edc8c4-efb2-4f18-9b53-76e4a2feb623?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) centered on allegations by former top deputies that he accepted bribes and abused the authority of his office to help a wealthy friend and campaign donor.

And while Paxton has remained under federal investigation for the same corruption allegations that formed the basis of his impeachment, that investigation [is now in the hands](https://substack.com/redirect/f7a509e4-8ccc-4e59-8c52-532b9f9b2bf0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of a Justice Department run by the administration of Paxton's stalwart political ally, President **Donald Trump**. The newly inaugurated president has nominated one of Paxton's former top aides,**Aaron Reitz** , to head the Justice Department's Office of Legal Policy.

In an election case that prompted the state bar lawsuits, Paxton and Webster sought to contest Biden's wins in four critical states: Georgia, Pennsylvania, Michigan and Wisconsin. The case revolved around unsubstantiated claims that those states made illegal pandemic-related changes to election procedures, allegedly casting the results into question.

The state bar suit against Paxton was sparked by public complaints from several lawyers and political activists who contended that Paxton had brought a frivolous lawsuit in bad faith and, in doing so, violated state disciplinary rules for attorneys.

In its decision to dismiss the lawsuit against Webster last month, the Texas Supreme Court upheld an earlier ruling by a Williamson County district judge who said that taking Webster's law license would violate the Texas Constitution's separation of powers doctrine. In their motion to drop Paxton's case, state bar lawyers acknowledged that their lawsuit against the attorney general "raises identical separation of powers issues" as those at issue in Webster's case." [Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/b7b7a5d9-398d-43f7-a3c1-a217d888f360?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

* **2025**

"**John Courage jumps back into San Antonio mayor's race,** " San Antonio Express-News' **Molly Smith** -- "[After dropping out of San Antonio's mayor's race late last year](https://substack.com/redirect/64c5b459-5874-46f2-90be-14c084a4df09?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), District 9 Councilman **John Courage** changed his mind Friday, putting his name on the May 3 ballot less than an hour before the deadline closed.

Days earlier, Courage, 73, told an attendee at a Northside Neighborhoods for Organized Development meeting that he ended his mayoral campaign because of [a health problem and the recent switch from two- to four-year terms](https://substack.com/redirect/1234c3b6-745a-4a89-a5cc-96fcee4b2a32?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for the mayor and City Council.

But on Friday morning, the last day of candidate filing, his campaign X account boiled with posts after more than a month of inactivity.

"Out of town $$$ is trying to buy their way into your City Hall," he wrote in one post. "It's outrageous that most of the money in this race are special interest groups from Austin, the RGV and the West Coast!"

That referred to money that City Hall outsiders **Gina Ortiz Jones** ,**Rolando Pablos** and **Beto Altamirano**[brought in during the second half of last year](https://substack.com/redirect/b54ffec4-b285-4b8f-a14f-964bfb78de54?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- fundraising that eclipsed what the mayoral contenders from City Council reported.

The post that followed took aim directly at Altamirano, who co-founded Irys Technologies, the company that designed the mobile app for the city's 311 system. "**Elon Musk** is canning thousands of Federal Civil Servants in our country. Now, another 'tech bro' wants to be your mayor...," it read.

Courage was the first to announce his mayoral bid in January 2024, almost a year before the candidate filing period for the May 3 election opened. But he began reconsidering his mayoral run around Halloween and told his supporters on Dec. 2 [that he was dropping out of the race](https://substack.com/redirect/1234c3b6-745a-4a89-a5cc-96fcee4b2a32?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

"While I remain passionate about public service, I must also acknowledge the realities of my age and health," Courage said at the time. "When I decided to run, I was 72 years old. By the time the next mayoral term concludes, I would be 78 years old. While I am fortunate to feel well today, I have encountered some health challenges that require greater attention to ensure my continued well-being."

He also cited his wife's recent retirement from city-owned CPS Energy, where she'd worked as a financial analyst.

He then [threw his support](https://substack.com/redirect/5ad5c66b-29f3-4173-aebf-4a10a8eeaafd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) behind Councilwoman **Adriana Rocha Garcia** , endorsing her campaign over those of fellow Council Members **Manny Pel aez **and **Melissa Cabello Havrda** and former Councilman **Clayton Perry**.

"There is no one that I've observed who works any harder or is any better informed and is so willing to put so much into their job than Councilwoman Rocha Garcia -- by far," he said." [SAEN](https://substack.com/redirect/b5ad182f-faab-4edb-a9be-ccb01b66a014?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

* **TEXANS IN DC**

"**Chip Roy blames DA after 7 San Antonio police officers shot,** " San Antonio Express-News' **Raul Trey Lopez** -- "Three weeks after a military veteran shot and wounded seven San Antonio police officers, a congressman is laying blame on the Bexar County District Attorney's bail policies -- and demanding that the Bexar County District Attorney address his concerns. The local police chief is also calling for change.

In a letter sent to Bexar County District Attorney **Joe Gonzales** , U.S. Rep. **Chip Roy** , a Republican whose 21st district includes a small part of San Antonio's North Side, raises several questions concerning bail policies.

"I write today with serious concerns regarding the recent incident in which an individual -- with a lengthy criminal history who was released on bail -- fired upon law enforcement, injuring seven San Antonio Police Department (SAPD) officers," Roy wrote in the Feb. 12 letter. "This incident appears to be another example where lax criminal justice policies, including bail reform, have threatened the safety of Bexar County residents and our law enforcement officers."

Gonzales has said his hands are tied regarding bail policy and that judges follow the law.

San Antonio Police Chief **William McManus** , who has similarly criticized Gonzales, a Democrat, told the San Antonio Express-News that crimes committed by individuals out on bond remain a safety concern in the city. McManus supports legislative initiatives for bail reform.

"We have all seen too many examples of violent criminals re-offending while released on bail. This is a major public and officer safety issue and a long-standing concern of mine," McManus said. "I support the current legislative efforts aimed at bail reform that provide judges with greater discretion to deny bail for violent offenders while ensuring community safety."

On Jan. 22, SAPD officers were called to the Sonterra Heights apartment complex in Stone Oak to respond to reports that a suicidal armed man -- later identified as Brandon Poulos -- was firing his weapon, SAPD officials said.

San Antonio police officers were beginning to coordinate their response when they were ambushed, according to a Feb. 2 custodial death report submitted to the Texas Attorney General's Office.

"As officers moved throughout the complex to find the shooting location, (Poulos) ambushed them, firing a round and striking an officer in the leg," the report said. "(Poulos) then retreated to a different area of the complex, where he hid from officers pointing a firearm at the police helicopter."

During an hours-long standoff, Poulos re-entered his apartment and shot at officers through windows, wounding six more officers, according to the report.

SWAT and negotiators used various tactics, trying to get Poulos to surrender. However, Poulos continued to threaten officers. Several SWAT officers fired at him, striking Poulos, a Navy veteran, multiple times. Poulos was pronounced dead at the scene.

Roy and McManus say Poulos should not have been released on bond following an alleged assault on Jan. 18. Poulos assaulted his wife and his wife's parents at a home in San Antonio, according to law enforcement officials and reports.

"This incident raises serious questions as to why someone with a history of violence was released on bail," Roy said.

Poulos was charged with assault causing bodily injury to a family member, two counts of injury to the elderly with the intent of serious bodily injury, and DWI.

A Bexar County judge then set bail at $35,000 on the criminal assault charges against Poulos. He was released from the Bexar County jail on Jan. 20 -- two days before the shooting, court records show.

After his release, Poulos' family obtained a protective order against him, prohibiting him from approaching their home in Fischer, north of Canyon Lake.

On Jan. 21, the day before Poulos shot and wounded the seven San Antonio police officers, Poulos violated a protective order, according to a Comal County official.

That night, Poulos had an hours-long standoff in Comal County in which he threatened sheriff's deputies; **Cary Zayas** , Comal County public information officer, wrote in a statement that included a timeline.

At 11:25 p.m., Poulos called the Comal County Sheriff's Office to report that he planned to commit suicide. He also said he wanted it "to be on record because his mother was trying to stop him," Zayas wrote.

Minutes later, a second emergency call was received from Poulos's mom, who was concerned about her adult son.

"She told the 911 operator the man was suicidal, intoxicated, and had fired a round from a firearm. The woman also informed deputies that her son had threatened to shoot law enforcement officers if they approached the home," according to the timeline.

Comal sheriff's deputies had a series of 30 calls with Poulos, who repeatedly stated that he did not want law enforcement involved, saying, "I will shoot police if they approach me."

Over four hours, two trained hostage negotiators worked with Poulos, who appeared highly intoxicated and agitated, making additional threats against law enforcement officers. "Despite efforts by negotiators to de-escalate the situation, the man refused to cooperate," Zayas wrote.

According to the timeline, a reverse 911 call--a robocall--was issued to nearby residents to alert them of the ongoing situation. Communication with Poulos stopped on Jan. 22 at about 3 a.m.

At 3:22 a.m., deputies deployed drones and were able to see that Poulos "hadn't attempted to leave home, and no further threats or shots were reported," officials said, adding that deputies maintained a presence in the area as a precaution.

By 4:00 p.m., Poulos reestablished contact with deputies but appeared "sober, apologetic and cooperative, admitting to being heavily intoxicated the previous night and recalling little of the incident," Zayas wrote. "He agreed to work with authorities on a surrender."

Later, deputies escorted Poulos' wife to return home. Poulos was no longer there. There were gunshot holes in the walls, and a car parked in the garage had a shattered window and shell casings from multiple firearms.

As Comal sheriff's deputies prepared to issue a "Be on the lookout," or BOLO, for Poulos, they discovered that he was allegedly responsible for shooting seven police officers in San Antonio later that night." [SAEN](https://substack.com/redirect/529bacb3-ee5d-4935-860a-2ccc451d7ec2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

"**Round Rock-based Dell reportedly close to $5 billion deal with Elon Musk's AI startup xAI,** " Austin American-Statesman's **Karoline Leonard** -- "Round Rock-based [Dell Technologies](https://substack.com/redirect/1aa8b2f7-c6d5-49dd-b77c-1be933b41f05?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is reportedly nearing a $5 billion deal to provide **Elon Musk's** artificial intelligence company with servers optimized for artificial intelligence work.

According to Bloomberg, the company will sell servers containing Nvidia Corp. semiconductors to xAI, Musk's AI startup, with delivery set for later this year. xAI's Grok assistant is already available on Musk's social media platform X, formerly known as Twitter.

Dell's shares jumped as much as 6% on Friday following the news.

Musk is building xAI's facilities in Memphis, Tennessee, although most of his businesses are located or moving their headquarters to Texas. Tesla, The Boring Company, SpaceX, and X all have facilities in Central Texas.

Dell's fourth-quarter earnings call is scheduled for later this month and AI server business is expected to be a major focus for investors. Some analysts expect Dell to have shipped more than $10 billion of AI servers in the fiscal year that ended last month." [AAS](https://substack.com/redirect/9944fa2d-3a12-4eba-8803-433ea374f424?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

"**Volaris to launch two new Mexican flights from San Antonio in July,** " San Antonio Express-News' **Megan Rodriguez** -- "Volaris will launch two new nonstop flights from San Antonio to the Mexican cities of Morelia and San Luis Potosi this summer.

The Mexican budget airline's new routes kick off on July 5 and will run three times a week on Tuesdays, Thursdays and Saturdays.

The service will give San Antonio International Airport a total of nine nonstop destinations in Mexico -- up from the four offerings available prior to the pandemic, airport officials said in a Friday news release.

Other Mexican destinations that airlines already fly to from San Antonio include Queretaro, Torreon, Cancun, Guadalajara, Leon, Mexico City and Monterrey.

"These new flights will not only make it easier to visit family and friends but also to grow business ties and continue to bring tourists to our beautiful region," said Airport Director **Jesus Saenz**.

Morelia and San Luis Potosi are fast-growing cities.

[Morelia's population](https://substack.com/redirect/62aa8eb1-1a00-41ac-8593-189b6f0eaaba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) grew by more than 16% between 2010 and 2020, its population climbing to 849,053. More than half of the city's $273 million of exports include figs, pineapples, avocados, guava and mangoes, according to 2023 data from the Mexican government.

[San Luis Potosi's population](https://substack.com/redirect/0db737ba-323e-4600-878c-60746a4c6e3b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) rose by 18% in the same period up to 911,908 people in 2020. Automotive parts are the city's main export, making up $1.68 billion of the city's nearly $8 billion worth of exports in 2023." TOP NEWS "Measles outbreak is Texas' largest in 30 years, " Dallas Morning News'Emily Brindley -- "The ongoing measles outbreak in Texas' South Plains region is now the largest the state has seen in more than three decades. The Texas Department of State Health Services reported on Friday there are now , largely among children. The case count includes 13 hospitalizations. The vast majority of the cases are from residents of Gaines County about 350 miles west of Dallas, where the outbreak appears to have originated. But there are now additional cases identified in the nearby counties of Lynn, Terry and Yoakum. Lara Anton , a spokesperson for the state health department, said the outbreak appears to have originated in a Mennonite community in Gaines County. The Mennonite church is not widely opposed to vaccination. Mennonite leaders have talked publicly about their COVID vaccinations, for instance, and numerous leaders did not support religious exemptions for COVID vaccines. All of the confirmed cases are among people who are either unvaccinated or whose vaccination status was unknown, according to the state health department. Measles is so contagious that during an outbreak "it's very hard to keep it from spreading in a community that has low vaccination rates," Anton said. The case count now surpasses the number of cases seen in a 2013 outbreak. In that outbreak, a person returned from a trip to Asia with the disease, and the cases spread . In total, Texas saw 27 measles cases in 2013, which was the highest yearly count in the state in two decades. The South Plains outbreak already has a higher case count than the state saw in all of 2013. Anton said the case count in the ongoing outbreak may still increase as more cases are identified in the coming days and weeks. The state health department is urging Texans to get their measles vaccination if they have not already. The two-dose vaccine regimen offers about against the highly contagious virus. People who are not vaccinated are highly likely to contract the virus if they are exposed to it. Measles is one of the most contagious viruses. The virus can live in the air and on surfaces for after an infected person has left -- meaning that unvaccinated people in particular can catch the illness even without being in the same room as an infected person." ($) #TXLEGE "Texas is poised to become a film haven -- but not without a fight," Texas Tribune'sPooja Salhotra and Dan Keemahill -- "A promise to make Texas the film capital of the world has left local creatives raring to cash in -- and preparing for a legislative battle. The Texas Senate has proposed injecting $498 million to revamp the state's film incentive program, a historic sum that rivals most other states and more than doubles the $200 million lawmakers plugged into the program during the last budget cycle. Film industry insiders, who have for years been fighting for a larger and more consistent funding stream, could hardly believe it when they heard the $498 million figure, a line item in the draft budget the Senate filed last month. According to Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick , the funding would include $48 million in grants for small films and television commercials and up to $450 million in new tax credits, contingent on a bill passing. "It's like we are in a 'stars aligned' period," said Brian Gannon , director of the Austin Film Commission. "There's alignment across industry, across government. Everyone is excited."" STATE GOVERNMENT "Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton announces investigation into Chinese AI company DeepSeek, " Austin American-Statesman'sKaroline Leonard -- ""Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton that he is investigating DeepSeek, a Chinese artificial intelligence company that recently made headlines and caused several American tech stocks to plunge. According to the announcement, Paxton informed DeepSeek that it violated the Texas Data Privacy and Security Act. The investigation focuses on privacy concerns related to the company's AI platforms and its claims that its AI model rivals OpenAI's. OpenAI, founded by CEO Sam Altman and tech billionaire Elon Musk in 2015, is known for the popular generative AI platform ChatGPT. Paxton has issued third-party civil investigative demands to Google and Apple, requesting their analysis of DeepSeek and the documentation that DeepSeek submitted for consumer access. Paxton's announcement alleges that DeepSeek is "no more than a proxy for the" Chinese Communist Party. "The United States and Texas will continue to be at the forefront of global AI innovation, and any CCP-aligned company that tries to undermine that dominance by violating the rights of Texans and illegally undercutting American technology companies will face the full force of the law," he said. Paxton's investigation comes after Texas Gov. Greg Abbott banned the use of DeepSeek and other Chinese-backed AI and social media apps on government-issued devices on Jan. 31. The proclamation directed the Texas Department of Public Safety and the Department of Information Resources to add six technologies -- DeepSeek, Lemon8, Moomoo, RedNote, Tiger Brokers, and Webull -- to the state's prohibited technologies list. This ban prevents state employees and contractors from downloading and using these apps on both personal and state-owned devices. After banning the short-form video social media app TikTok on government-issued devices back in 2022, Abbott signed Senate Bill 1893, which granted him the authority to ban any social media applications or services that pose potential security risks to Texas." ($) "Texas Supreme Court dismisses state bar 's lawsuit against Ken Paxton for challenging 2020 presidential election," Texas Tribune'sJasper Scherer -- "The Texas Supreme Court on Friday dismissed the State Bar of Texas' lawsuit against Attorney General Ken Paxton for his attempt to overturn the results of the 2020 presidential election, extending for the once-embattled Republican leader. The decision came three weeks after lawyers with the bar's Commission for Lawyer Discipline asked for the dismissal, citing the high court's December against Paxton's top aide, Brent Webster , for working with Paxton to challenge the 2020 outcome in battleground states won by Democrat Joe Biden. An unsigned opinion from the all-Republican court acknowledged the two cases were "nearly identical" and agreed that the bar's move to voluntarily dismiss the lawsuit "renders this case moot." The state bar had sought to sanction Paxton, which could have carried a punishment ranging from a private reprimand to disbarment. Lawyers from the bar -- which regulates law licenses in Texas -- have argued that Paxton, in falsely claiming to have uncovered major evidence of election wrongdoing, forced the battleground states "to expend time, money, and resources to respond to the misrepresentations and false statements." The dismissal ends a case that . It is Paxton's second personal** legal victory within the last year: In March, prosecutors against Paxton under an agreement that required the attorney general to perform 100 hours of community service and take 15 hours of legal ethics courses. Paxton also agreed to pay around $271,000 in restitution to those he was accused of defrauding more than a decade ago when he allegedly solicited investors in a McKinney technology company without disclosing that the firm was paying him to promote its stock. Months before the securities fraud case was resolved, Paxton also centered on allegations by former top deputies that he accepted bribes and abused the authority of his office to help a wealthy friend and campaign donor. And while Paxton has remained under federal investigation for the same corruption allegations that formed the basis of his impeachment, that investigation of a Justice Department run by the administration of Paxton's stalwart political ally, President Donald Trump. The newly inaugurated president has nominated one of Paxton's former top aides,Aaron Reitz , to head the Justice Department's Office of Legal Policy. In an election case that prompted the state bar lawsuits, Paxton and Webster sought to contest Biden's wins in four critical states: Georgia, Pennsylvania, Michigan and Wisconsin. The case revolved around unsubstantiated claims that those states made illegal pandemic-related changes to election procedures, allegedly casting the results into question. The state bar suit against Paxton was sparked by public complaints from several lawyers and political activists who contended that Paxton had brought a frivolous lawsuit in bad faith and, in doing so, violated state disciplinary rules for attorneys. In its decision to dismiss the lawsuit against Webster last month, the Texas Supreme Court upheld an earlier ruling by a Williamson County district judge who said that taking Webster's law license would violate the Texas Constitution's separation of powers doctrine. In their motion to drop Paxton's case, state bar lawyers acknowledged that their lawsuit against the attorney general "raises identical separation of powers issues" as those at issue in Webster's case." 2025 "John Courage jumps back into San Antonio mayor's race, " San Antonio Express-News' Molly Smith -- ", District 9 Councilman John Courage changed his mind Friday, putting his name on the May 3 ballot less than an hour before the deadline closed. Days earlier, Courage, 73, told an attendee at a Northside Neighborhoods for Organized Development meeting that he ended his mayoral campaign because of for the mayor and City Council. But on Friday morning, the last day of candidate filing, his campaign X account boiled with posts after more than a month of inactivity. "Out of town $$$ is trying to buy their way into your City Hall," he wrote in one post. "It's outrageous that most of the money in this race are special interest groups from Austin, the RGV and the West Coast!" That referred to money that City Hall outsiders Gina Ortiz Jones ,Rolando Pablos and Beto Altamirano -- fundraising that eclipsed what the mayoral contenders from City Council reported. The post that followed took aim directly at Altamirano, who co-founded Irys Technologies, the company that designed the mobile app for the city's 311 system. "Elon Musk is canning thousands of Federal Civil Servants in our country. Now, another 'tech bro' wants to be your mayor...," it read. Courage was the first to announce his mayoral bid in January 2024, almost a year before the candidate filing period for the May 3 election opened. But he began reconsidering his mayoral run around Halloween and told his supporters on Dec. 2 . "While I remain passionate about public service, I must also acknowledge the realities of my age and health," Courage said at the time. "When I decided to run, I was 72 years old. By the time the next mayoral term concludes, I would be 78 years old. While I am fortunate to feel well today, I have encountered some health challenges that require greater attention to ensure my continued well-being." He also cited his wife's recent retirement from city-owned CPS Energy, where she'd worked as a financial analyst. He then behind Councilwoman Adriana Rocha Garcia , endorsing her campaign over those of fellow Council Members Manny Pel aez and Melissa Cabello Havrda and former Councilman Clayton Perry. "There is no one that I've observed who works any harder or is any better informed and is so willing to put so much into their job than Councilwoman Rocha Garcia -- by far," he said." ($) TEXANS IN DC "Chip Roy blames DA after 7 San Antonio police officers shot, " San Antonio Express-News' Raul Trey Lopez -- "Three weeks after a military veteran shot and wounded seven San Antonio police officers, a congressman is laying blame on the Bexar County District Attorney's bail policies -- and demanding that the Bexar County District Attorney address his concerns. The local police chief is also calling for change. In a letter sent to Bexar County District Attorney Joe Gonzales , U.S. Rep. Chip Roy , a Republican whose 21st district includes a small part of San Antonio's North Side, raises several questions concerning bail policies. "I write today with serious concerns regarding the recent incident in which an individual -- with a lengthy criminal history who was released on bail -- fired upon law enforcement, injuring seven San Antonio Police Department (SAPD) officers," Roy wrote in the Feb. 12 letter. "This incident appears to be another example where lax criminal justice policies, including bail reform, have threatened the safety of Bexar County residents and our law enforcement officers." Gonzales has said his hands are tied regarding bail policy and that judges follow the law. San Antonio Police Chief William McManus , who has similarly criticized Gonzales, a Democrat, told the San Antonio Express-News that crimes committed by individuals out on bond remain a safety concern in the city. McManus supports legislative initiatives for bail reform. "We have all seen too many examples of violent criminals re-offending while released on bail. This is a major public and officer safety issue and a long-standing concern of mine," McManus said. "I support the current legislative efforts aimed at bail reform that provide judges with greater discretion to deny bail for violent offenders while ensuring community safety." On Jan. 22, SAPD officers were called to the Sonterra Heights apartment complex in Stone Oak to respond to reports that a suicidal armed man -- later identified as Brandon Poulos -- was firing his weapon, SAPD officials said. San Antonio police officers were beginning to coordinate their response when they were ambushed, according to a Feb. 2 custodial death report submitted to the Texas Attorney General's Office. "As officers moved throughout the complex to find the shooting location, (Poulos) ambushed them, firing a round and striking an officer in the leg," the report said. "(Poulos) then retreated to a different area of the complex, where he hid from officers pointing a firearm at the police helicopter." During an hours-long standoff, Poulos re-entered his apartment and shot at officers through windows, wounding six more officers, according to the report. SWAT and negotiators used various tactics, trying to get Poulos to surrender. However, Poulos continued to threaten officers. Several SWAT officers fired at him, striking Poulos, a Navy veteran, multiple times. Poulos was pronounced dead at the scene. Roy and McManus say Poulos should not have been released on bond following an alleged assault on Jan. 18. Poulos assaulted his wife and his wife's parents at a home in San Antonio, according to law enforcement officials and reports. "This incident raises serious questions as to why someone with a history of violence was released on bail," Roy said. Poulos was charged with assault causing bodily injury to a family member, two counts of injury to the elderly with the intent of serious bodily injury, and DWI. A Bexar County judge then set bail at $35,000 on the criminal assault charges against Poulos. He was released from the Bexar County jail on Jan. 20 -- two days before the shooting, court records show. After his release, Poulos' family obtained a protective order against him, prohibiting him from approaching their home in Fischer, north of Canyon Lake. On Jan. 21, the day before Poulos shot and wounded the seven San Antonio police officers, Poulos violated a protective order, according to a Comal County official. That night, Poulos had an hours-long standoff in Comal County in which he threatened sheriff's deputies; Cary Zayas , Comal County public information officer, wrote in a statement that included a timeline. At 11:25 p.m., Poulos called the Comal County Sheriff's Office to report that he planned to commit suicide. He also said he wanted it "to be on record because his mother was trying to stop him," Zayas wrote. Minutes later, a second emergency call was received from Poulos's mom, who was concerned about her adult son. "She told the 911 operator the man was suicidal, intoxicated, and had fired a round from a firearm. The woman also informed deputies that her son had threatened to shoot law enforcement officers if they approached the home," according to the timeline. Comal sheriff's deputies had a series of 30 calls with Poulos, who repeatedly stated that he did not want law enforcement involved, saying, "I will shoot police if they approach me." Over four hours, two trained hostage negotiators worked with Poulos, who appeared highly intoxicated and agitated, making additional threats against law enforcement officers. "Despite efforts by negotiators to de-escalate the situation, the man refused to cooperate," Zayas wrote. According to the timeline, a reverse 911 call--a robocall--was issued to nearby residents to alert them of the ongoing situation. Communication with Poulos stopped on Jan. 22 at about 3 a.m. At 3:22 a.m., deputies deployed drones and were able to see that Poulos "hadn't attempted to leave home, and no further threats or shots were reported," officials said, adding that deputies maintained a presence in the area as a precaution. By 4:00 p.m., Poulos reestablished contact with deputies but appeared "sober, apologetic and cooperative, admitting to being heavily intoxicated the previous night and recalling little of the incident," Zayas wrote. "He agreed to work with authorities on a surrender." Later, deputies escorted Poulos' wife to return home. Poulos was no longer there. There were gunshot holes in the walls, and a car parked in the garage had a shattered window and shell casings from multiple firearms. As Comal sheriff's deputies prepared to issue a "Be on the lookout," or BOLO, for Poulos, they discovered that he was allegedly responsible for shooting seven police officers in San Antonio later that night." ($) BUSINESS NEWS "Round Rock-based Dell reportedly close to $5 billion deal with Elon Musk's AI startup xAI, " Austin American-Statesman's Karoline Leonard -- "Round Rock-based is reportedly nearing a $5 billion deal to provide Elon Musk's artificial intelligence company with servers optimized for artificial intelligence work. According to Bloomberg, the company will sell servers containing Nvidia Corp. semiconductors to xAI, Musk's AI startup, with delivery set for later this year. xAI's Grok assistant is already available on Musk's social media platform X, formerly known as Twitter. Dell's shares jumped as much as 6% on Friday following the news. Musk is building xAI's facilities in Memphis, Tennessee, although most of his businesses are located or moving their headquarters to Texas. Tesla, The Boring Company, SpaceX, and X all have facilities in Central Texas. Dell's fourth-quarter earnings call is scheduled for later this month and AI server business is expected to be a major focus for investors. Some analysts expect Dell to have shipped more than $10 billion of AI servers in the fiscal year that ended last month." ($) "Volaris to launch two new Mexican flights from San Antonio in July, " San Antonio Express-News' Megan Rodriguez -- "Volaris will launch two new nonstop flights from San Antonio to the Mexican cities of Morelia and San Luis Potosi this summer. The Mexican budget airline's new routes kick off on July 5 and will run three times a week on Tuesdays, Thursdays and Saturdays. The service will give San Antonio International Airport a total of nine nonstop destinations in Mexico -- up from the four offerings available prior to the pandemic, airport officials said in a Friday news release. Other Mexican destinations that airlines already fly to from San Antonio include Queretaro, Torreon, Cancun, Guadalajara, Leon, Mexico City and Monterrey. "These new flights will not only make it easier to visit family and friends but also to grow business ties and continue to bring tourists to our beautiful region," said Airport Director Jesus Saenz. Morelia and San Luis Potosi are fast-growing cities. grew by more than 16% between 2010 and 2020, its population climbing to 849,053. More than half of the city's $273 million of exports include figs, pineapples, avocados, guava and mangoes, according to 2023 data from the Mexican government. rose by 18% in the same period up to 911,908 people in 2020. Automotive parts are the city's main export, making up $1.68 billion of the city's nearly $8 billion worth of exports in 2023." Survivors and supporters hope lawmakers will take action. Few employers are signing up " TX TRIB: "Texas migrant shelters are nearly empty after Trump 's actions effectively shut the border" TX TRIB: "U.S. claims migrants held at Guantanamo are "worst of the worst." **AUSTIN NEWSLETTER LAUNCHED**
Are you one of the 100,000+ subscribers to [ATX Pulse](https://substack.com/redirect/4ec8cbd5-aaf7-43ed-b696-ab25e4028eab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a newsletter that delivers EVERYTHING you NEED TO KNOW about Austin?

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/cf56d62f-ad92-4f2a-a405-468cb292881b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
