clean_body: Here's What You Need to Know in Texas Today. | | | February 15, 2025 | | Here's the PAID subscriber version of the Must Read Texas daily email -- so you can see what you're missing. Become a PAID subscriber for $7/mo or $70/yr and SAVE TIME and be BETTER INFORMED -- mustreadtx.substack.com. | | – BY: | FRIDAY || 2/14/25 | Good Friday evening. Thank you for being a paid subscriber. | “If something of importance is known in Texas, Matt knows it. With a decline in the number of credible news organizations, the Must Read Texas morning email is indispensable for anyone that wants to continue to be informed.” – Former U.S. Senator Phil Gramm (R-TX) | TOP NEWS “Measles outbreak is Texas ' largest in 30 years,” Dallas Morning News'Emily Brindley — “The ongoing measles outbreak in Texas’ South Plains region is now the largest the state has seen in more than three decades. The Texas Department of State Health Services reported on Friday there are now , largely among children. The case count includes 13 hospitalizations. The vast majority of the cases are from residents of Gaines County about 350 miles west of Dallas, where the outbreak appears to have originated. But there are now additional cases identified in the nearby counties of Lynn, Terry and Yoakum. Lara Anton , a spokesperson for the state health department, said the outbreak appears to have originated in a Mennonite community in Gaines County. The Mennonite church is not widely opposed to vaccination. Mennonite leaders have talked publicly about their COVID vaccinations, for instance, and numerous leaders did not support religious exemptions for COVID vaccines. » » All of the confirmed cases are among people who are either unvaccinated or whose vaccination status was unknown, according to the state health department. Measles is so contagious that during an outbreak “it’s very hard to keep it from spreading in a community that has low vaccination rates,” Anton said. The case count now surpasses the number of cases seen in a 2013 outbreak. In that outbreak, a person returned from a trip to Asia with the disease, and the cases spread . In total, Texas saw 27 measles cases in 2013, which was the highest yearly count in the state in two decades. The South Plains outbreak already has a higher case count than the state saw in all of 2013. Anton said the case count in the ongoing outbreak may still increase as more cases are identified in the coming days and weeks. The state health department is urging Texans to get their measles vaccination if they have not already. The two-dose vaccine regimen offers about against the highly contagious virus. People who are not vaccinated are highly likely to contract the virus if they are exposed to it. Measles is one of the most contagious viruses. The virus can live in the air and on surfaces for after an infected person has left — meaning that unvaccinated people in particular can catch the illness even without being in the same room as an infected person." ($) | #TXLEGE “Texas is poised to become a film haven — but not without a fight, ” Texas Tribune'sPooja Salhotra and Dan Keemahill — “A promise to make Texas the film capital of the world has left local creatives raring to cash in — and preparing for a legislative battle. The Texas Senate has proposed injecting $498 million to revamp the state’s film incentive program, a historic sum that rivals most other states and more than doubles the $200 million lawmakers plugged into the program during the last budget cycle. Film industry insiders, who have for years been fighting for a larger and more consistent funding stream, could hardly believe it when they heard the $498 million figure, a line item in the draft budget the Senate filed last month. According to Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick , the funding would include $48 million in grants for small films and television commercials and up to $450 million in new tax credits, contingent on a bill passing. “It’s like we are in a ‘stars aligned’ period,” said Brian Gannon , director of the Austin Film Commission. “There’s alignment across industry, across government. Everyone is excited.”" | STATE GOVERNMENT “Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton announces investigation into Chinese AI company DeepSeek, ” Austin American-Statesman'sKaroline Leonard — “"Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton that he is investigating DeepSeek, a Chinese artificial intelligence company that recently made headlines and caused several American tech stocks to plunge. According to the announcement, Paxton informed DeepSeek that it violated the Texas Data Privacy and Security Act. The investigation focuses on privacy concerns related to the company's AI platforms and its claims that its AI model rivals OpenAI's. OpenAI, founded by CEO Sam Altman and tech billionaire Elon Musk in 2015, is known for the popular generative AI platform ChatGPT. Paxton has issued third-party civil investigative demands to Google and Apple, requesting their analysis of DeepSeek and the documentation that DeepSeek submitted for consumer access. Paxton's announcement alleges that DeepSeek is "no more than a proxy for the" Chinese Communist Party. “The United States and Texas will continue to be at the forefront of global AI innovation, and any CCP-aligned company that tries to undermine that dominance by violating the rights of Texans and illegally undercutting American technology companies will face the full force of the law," he said. Paxton's investigation comes after Texas Gov. Greg Abbott banned the use of DeepSeek and other Chinese-backed AI and social media apps on government-issued devices on Jan. 31. The proclamation directed the Texas Department of Public Safety and the Department of Information Resources to add six technologies — DeepSeek, Lemon8, Moomoo, RedNote, Tiger Brokers, and Webull — to the state's prohibited technologies list. This ban prevents state employees and contractors from downloading and using these apps on both personal and state-owned devices. After banning the short-form video social media app TikTok on government-issued devices back in 2022, Abbott signed Senate Bill 1893, which granted him the authority to ban any social media applications or services that pose potential security risks to Texas." ($) “Texas Supreme Court dismisses state bar’s lawsuit against Ken Paxton for challenging 2020 presidential election, ” Texas Tribune'sJasper Scherer — “The Texas Supreme Court on Friday dismissed the State Bar of Texas' lawsuit against Attorney General Ken Paxton for his attempt to overturn the results of the 2020 presidential election, extending for the once-embattled Republican leader. The decision came three weeks after lawyers with the bar’s Commission for Lawyer Discipline asked for the dismissal, citing the high court’s December against Paxton’s top aide, Brent Webster , for working with Paxton to challenge the 2020 outcome in battleground states won by Democrat Joe Biden. An unsigned opinion from the all-Republican court acknowledged the two cases were "nearly identical" and agreed that the bar's move to voluntarily dismiss the lawsuit "renders this case moot." The state bar had sought to sanction Paxton, which could have carried a punishment ranging from a private reprimand to disbarment. Lawyers from the bar — which regulates law licenses in Texas — have argued that Paxton, in falsely claiming to have uncovered major evidence of election wrongdoing, forced the battleground states “to expend time, money, and resources to respond to the misrepresentations and false statements.” The dismissal ends a case that . It is Paxton’s second personal legal victory within the last year: In March, prosecutors against Paxton under an agreement that required the attorney general to perform 100 hours of community service and take 15 hours of legal ethics courses. Paxton also agreed to pay around $271,000 in restitution to those he was accused of defrauding more than a decade ago when he allegedly solicited investors in a McKinney technology company without disclosing that the firm was paying him to promote its stock. Months before the securities fraud case was resolved, Paxton also centered on allegations by former top deputies that he accepted bribes and abused the authority of his office to help a wealthy friend and campaign donor. And while Paxton has remained under federal investigation for the same corruption allegations that formed the basis of his impeachment, that investigation of a Justice Department run by the administration of Paxton’s stalwart political ally, President Donald Trump. The newly inaugurated president has nominated one of Paxton’s former top aides,Aaron Reitz , to head the Justice Department’s Office of Legal Policy. In an election case that prompted the state bar lawsuits, Paxton and Webster sought to contest Biden’s wins in four critical states: Georgia, Pennsylvania, Michigan and Wisconsin. The case revolved around unsubstantiated claims that those states made illegal pandemic-related changes to election procedures, allegedly casting the results into question. The state bar suit against Paxton was sparked by public complaints from several lawyers and political activists who contended that Paxton had brought a frivolous lawsuit in bad faith and, in doing so, violated state disciplinary rules for attorneys. In its decision to dismiss the lawsuit against Webster last month, the Texas Supreme Court upheld an earlier ruling by a Williamson County district judge who said that taking Webster’s law license would violate the Texas Constitution’s separation of powers doctrine. In their motion to drop Paxton’s case, state bar lawyers acknowledged that their lawsuit against the attorney general “raises identical separation of powers issues” as those at issue in Webster’s case." | 2025 “John Courage jumps back into San Antonio mayor 's race,” San Antonio Express-News' Molly Smith — “, District 9 Councilman John Courage changed his mind Friday, putting his name on the May 3 ballot less than an hour before the deadline closed. Days earlier, Courage, 73, told an attendee at a Northside Neighborhoods for Organized Development meeting that he ended his mayoral campaign because of for the mayor and City Council. But on Friday morning, the last day of candidate filing, his campaign X account boiled with posts after more than a month of inactivity. “Out of town $$$ is trying to buy their way into your City Hall,” he wrote in one post. “It’s outrageous that most of the money in this race are special interest groups from Austin, the RGV and the West Coast!” That referred to money that City Hall outsiders Gina Ortiz Jones ,Rolando Pablos and Beto Altamirano — fundraising that eclipsed what the mayoral contenders from City Council reported. The post that followed took aim directly at Altamirano, who co-founded Irys Technologies, the company that designed the mobile app for the city’s 311 system. “Elon Musk is canning thousands of Federal Civil Servants in our country. Now, another ‘tech bro’ wants to be your mayor...,” it read. Courage was the first to announce his mayoral bid in January 2024, almost a year before the candidate filing period for the May 3 election opened. But he began reconsidering his mayoral run around Halloween and told his supporters on Dec. 2 . “While I remain passionate about public service, I must also acknowledge the realities of my age and health,” Courage said at the time. “When I decided to run, I was 72 years old. By the time the next mayoral term concludes, I would be 78 years old. While I am fortunate to feel well today, I have encountered some health challenges that require greater attention to ensure my continued well-being.” He also cited his wife’s recent retirement from city-owned CPS Energy, where she’d worked as a financial analyst. He then behind Councilwoman Adriana Rocha Garcia , endorsing her campaign over those of fellow Council Members Manny Peláez and Melissa Cabello Havrda and former Councilman Clayton Perry. “There is no one that I’ve observed who works any harder or is any better informed and is so willing to put so much into their job than Councilwoman Rocha Garcia — by far,” he said." ($) | TEXANS IN DC “Chip Roy blames DA after 7 San Antonio police officers shot, ” San Antonio Express-News' Raul Trey Lopez — “Three weeks after a military veteran shot and wounded seven San Antonio police officers, a congressman is laying blame on the Bexar County District Attorney's bail policies — and demanding that the Bexar County District Attorney address his concerns. The local police chief is also calling for change. In a letter sent to Bexar County District Attorney Joe Gonzales , U.S. Rep. Chip Roy , a Republican whose 21st district includes a small part of San Antonio's North Side, raises several questions concerning bail policies. "I write today with serious concerns regarding the recent incident in which an individual — with a lengthy criminal history who was released on bail — fired upon law enforcement, injuring seven San Antonio Police Department (SAPD) officers," Roy wrote in the Feb. 12 letter. "This incident appears to be another example where lax criminal justice policies, including bail reform, have threatened the safety of Bexar County residents and our law enforcement officers." Gonzales has said his hands are tied regarding bail policy and that judges follow the law. San Antonio Police Chief William McManus , who has similarly criticized Gonzales, a Democrat, told the San Antonio Express-News that crimes committed by individuals out on bond remain a safety concern in the city. McManus supports legislative initiatives for bail reform. "We have all seen too many examples of violent criminals re-offending while released on bail. This is a major public and officer safety issue and a long-standing concern of mine," McManus said. "I support the current legislative efforts aimed at bail reform that provide judges with greater discretion to deny bail for violent offenders while ensuring community safety." On Jan. 22, SAPD officers were called to the Sonterra Heights apartment complex in Stone Oak to respond to reports that a suicidal armed man — later identified as Brandon Poulos — was firing his weapon, SAPD officials said. San Antonio police officers were beginning to coordinate their response when they were ambushed, according to a Feb. 2 custodial death report submitted to the Texas Attorney General's Office. "As officers moved throughout the complex to find the shooting location, (Poulos) ambushed them, firing a round and striking an officer in the leg," the report said. "(Poulos) then retreated to a different area of the complex, where he hid from officers pointing a firearm at the police helicopter." During an hours-long standoff, Poulos re-entered his apartment and shot at officers through windows, wounding six more officers, according to the report. SWAT and negotiators used various tactics, trying to get Poulos to surrender. However, Poulos continued to threaten officers. Several SWAT officers fired at him, striking Poulos, a Navy veteran, multiple times. Poulos was pronounced dead at the scene. Roy and McManus say Poulos should not have been released on bond following an alleged assault on Jan. 18. Poulos assaulted his wife and his wife's parents at a home in San Antonio, according to law enforcement officials and reports. "This incident raises serious questions as to why someone with a history of violence was released on bail," Roy said. Poulos was charged with assault causing bodily injury to a family member, two counts of injury to the elderly with the intent of serious bodily injury, and DWI. A Bexar County judge then set bail at $35,000 on the criminal assault charges against Poulos. He was released from the Bexar County jail on Jan. 20 — two days before the shooting, court records show. After his release, Poulos' family obtained a protective order against him, prohibiting him from approaching their home in Fischer, north of Canyon Lake. On Jan. 21, the day before Poulos shot and wounded the seven San Antonio police officers, Poulos violated a protective order, according to a Comal County official. That night, Poulos had an hours-long standoff in Comal County in which he threatened sheriff's deputies; Cary Zayas , Comal County public information officer, wrote in a statement that included a timeline. At 11:25 p.m., Poulos called the Comal County Sheriff's Office to report that he planned to commit suicide. He also said he wanted it "to be on record because his mother was trying to stop him," Zayas wrote. Minutes later, a second emergency call was received from Poulos's mom, who was concerned about her adult son. "She told the 911 operator the man was suicidal, intoxicated, and had fired a round from a firearm. The woman also informed deputies that her son had threatened to shoot law enforcement officers if they approached the home," according to the timeline. Comal sheriff's deputies had a series of 30 calls with Poulos, who repeatedly stated that he did not want law enforcement involved, saying, "I will shoot police if they approach me." Over four hours, two trained hostage negotiators worked with Poulos, who appeared highly intoxicated and agitated, making additional threats against law enforcement officers. "Despite efforts by negotiators to de-escalate the situation, the man refused to cooperate," Zayas wrote. According to the timeline, a reverse 911 call—a robocall—was issued to nearby residents to alert them of the ongoing situation. Communication with Poulos stopped on Jan. 22 at about 3 a.m. At 3:22 a.m., deputies deployed drones and were able to see that Poulos "hadn't attempted to leave home, and no further threats or shots were reported," officials said, adding that deputies maintained a presence in the area as a precaution. By 4:00 p.m., Poulos reestablished contact with deputies but appeared "sober, apologetic and cooperative, admitting to being heavily intoxicated the previous night and recalling little of the incident," Zayas wrote. "He agreed to work with authorities on a surrender." Later, deputies escorted Poulos' wife to return home. Poulos was no longer there. There were gunshot holes in the walls, and a car parked in the garage had a shattered window and shell casings from multiple firearms. As Comal sheriff's deputies prepared to issue a "Be on the lookout," or BOLO, for Poulos, they discovered that he was allegedly responsible for shooting seven police officers in San Antonio later that night." ($) | BUSINESS NEWS “Round Rock-based Dell reportedly close to $5 billion deal with Elon Musk 's AI startup xAI,” Austin American-Statesman's Karoline Leonard — “Round Rock-based is reportedly nearing a $5 billion deal to provide Elon Musk 's artificial intelligence company with servers optimized for artificial intelligence work. According to Bloomberg, the company will sell servers containing Nvidia Corp. semiconductors to xAI, Musk's AI startup, with delivery set for later this year. xAI's Grok assistant is already available on Musk's social media platform X, formerly known as Twitter. Dell's shares jumped as much as 6% on Friday following the news. Musk is building xAI's facilities in Memphis, Tennessee, although most of his businesses are located or moving their headquarters to Texas. Tesla, The Boring Company, SpaceX, and X all have facilities in Central Texas. Dell's fourth-quarter earnings call is scheduled for later this month and AI server business is expected to be a major focus for investors. Some analysts expect Dell to have shipped more than $10 billion of AI servers in the fiscal year that ended last month." ($) “Volaris to launch two new Mexican flights from San Antonio in July, ” San Antonio Express-News' Megan Rodriguez — “Volaris will launch two new nonstop flights from San Antonio to the Mexican cities of Morelia and San Luis Potosí this summer. The Mexican budget airline’s new routes kick off on July 5 and will run three times a week on Tuesdays, Thursdays and Saturdays. The service will give San Antonio International Airport a total of nine nonstop destinations in Mexico — up from the four offerings available prior to the pandemic, airport officials said in a Friday news release. Other Mexican destinations that airlines already fly to from San Antonio include Querétaro, Torreón, Cancun, Guadalajara, Leon, Mexico City and Monterrey. “These new flights will not only make it easier to visit family and friends but also to grow business ties and continue to bring tourists to our beautiful region,” said Airport Director Jesus Saenz. Morelia and San Luis Potosí are fast-growing cities. grew by more than 16% between 2010 and 2020, its population climbing to 849,053. More than half of the city’s $273 million of exports include figs, pineapples, avocados, guava and mangoes, according to 2023 data from the Mexican government. rose by 18% in the same period up to 911,908 people in 2020. Automotive parts are the city’s main export, making up $1.68 billion of the city’s nearly $8 billion worth of exports in 2023." name: Gay Valimont
subject: Remember Inflation? I DO.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Seth Moulton for Congress
body:
Fellow Democrat:

Remember inflation? It was supposedly the central focus of Donald Trump and MAGA Republicans, who promised ON DAY ONE that inflation would all but go away.

But ON DAY ONE, they pardoned January 6th terrorists and since then it’s been a carnival for ketamine-using billionaires and special interests, renaming bodies of water, seeking vengeance against the FBI and starting trade wars that are only going to make things worse.

I’m Gay Valimont, the Democratic nominee for Florida’s First Congressional Seat that’s vacant because Matt Gaetz took a very public dive. While HE went down, inflation hasn’t. That’s why I need your help.

We have a special election on April 1st - less than 50 days away - and if we flip this seat and two other special election seats up for the vote - Democrats could RETAKE THE HOUSE in 2025 - and start making laws to LOWER inflation.

Can you help my campaign for working families by splitting a contribution between my campaign and Seth Moulton. Can you do $5? Or $15? Or $30? Or MORE? [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/m26-valimont]

Donate [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/m26-valimont]In my race to replace Matt Gaetz, I’ve outlined several steps I’d take to help Floridians in the Panhandle: RAISE THE WAGE FOR ENLISTED FAMILIES. Protect the Affordable Care Act to keep costs low. Expand supports for child care and home healthcare. AND NO to stupid trade wars.

I know what it’s like to struggle with medical bills - and to make ends meet. I’ve heard the voices all over the 1st District: They gave Trump and MAGA Republicans a chance, thinking - HOPING - that they’d really do what they said and focus on the economy.

That’s why I need your help to deliver a real VICTORY for Democrats on April 1. Can I count on you to split a donation between my campaign and Seth Moulton right now to DEFEAT MAGA Extremism? [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/m26-valimont]

Gay Valimont

Democratic Nominee (FL-01)

https://secure.actblue.com/donate/m26-valimont [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/m26-valimont]

name: Doug Lindner, LCV
subject: [URGENT]: Trump’s dangerous FBI nominee up for a vote in just days
body:
To view this mail in a browser, copy https://links.messages.lcv.org/servlet/MailView?ms=MTIxMzkxMTIS1&r=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&j=MjE2MDIyMDIzNAS2&mt=1&rt=0 into your browser.
TELL SENATORS TO VOTE NO ON KASH PATEL FOR FBI

Kash Patel, Trump’s nominee for FBI Director, is an extreme loyalist who’s said
he’ll use the powers of the FBI to come after Trump’s political enemies. He’s
put the public “on notice” and committed to “come after the people in the
media.”

His confirmation isn’t a sure thing — he’s stirred up enough controversy that we
have a real chanceto convince enough senators to vote no. Public outrage already forced Matt Gaetz
to withdraw from a potential nomination — we can do it again, if people like you
make your voices heard.

Take action today: send a message to your senators urging them to vote against Patel’s nomination >> https://links.messages.lcv. TAKE ACTION NOW https://links.messages.lcv. Peter, another of Trump’s most extreme nominees will be up for a vote
in the Senate next week — and if he’s confirmed, our democracy and our movement
to protect people and planet could be at stake: This Thursday, Republicans on the Senate Judiciary Committee voted to advance
the nomination of Kash Patel, Donald Trump’s pick for FBI Director. Patel is no ordinary nominee. He’s an extremist who has already announced his intention to weaponize the FBI against Trump’s perceived
enemies. Patel could be up for a final confirmation vote in the Senate as soon as Tuesday
— which means we only have a few days to stop it from going through. His
nomination has already been roiled in controversy, including allegations that he
lied under oath before the Senate Judiciary Committee about his role in FBI
political purges. We have a genuine chance of stopping him, but only if people like you send a clear message that his nomination is
unacceptable. Tell your senators TODAY to vote NO on Kash Patel for FBI Director >> https://links.messages.lcv. Patel is on the record: “We will go out and find the conspirators, not just in
government but in the media. Yes, we’re going to come after the people in the
media who lied about American citizens, who helped Joe Biden rig presidential
elections — we’re going to come after you. Whether it’s criminally or civilly,
we’ll figure that out, but yeah — we’re putting you all on notice.” These are the words of someone who would serve Trump and his billionaire cronies
by targeting their political enemies. And as the Trump administration’s actions
of the last month have made clear, people fighting for crucial environmental
protections could absolutely end up in their list of targets. Patel’s commitment to use the powers of federal law enforcement to target the
media and those who disagree with the extreme Trump agenda is a major threat to
our freedoms — we need to send an unmistakable message to the Senate that Kash
Patel is not qualified to lead the FBI. Please send a message to your senators TODAY urging them to vote NO on Patel’s nomination as FBI Director >> https://links.messages.lcv. We’re committed to continue fighting to protect our democracy and our
communities. We hope you are too — thanks for taking action. Doug Lindner
Senior Director of Judiciary and Democracy
League of Conservation Voters This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you,update your contact information https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/ContactInformation?spMailingID=12139112&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MjE2MDIyMDIzNAS2&spReportId=MjE2MDIyMDIzNAS2.Click here to unsubscribe https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/Unsubscribe?spMailingID=12139112&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MjE2MDIyMDIzNAS2&spReportId=MjE2MDIyMDIzNAS2 from our supporter list.Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback here mailto:feedback@lcv.org?subject=, or just reply to this email! Thanks for your support.

740 15th St NW, Suite 700
Washington, DC 20005
202-785-8683 DONATE https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/Zzb0MqwbaLc_

Gay Valimont here, FL-1 candidate, and I am **shocked:** In a striking turn of events, former Senate GOP leader **Mitch McConnell has broken ranks** with his party to oppose President Trump's controversial Cabinet nominees, including Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard, and Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. McConnell's dissent highlights the deepening fractures within the Republican Party and underscores the urgent need for principled leadership. **With your help, we can take advantage of this fragmented Republican Party before April 1st special elections.**
---
---
This internal discord is more than political theater; it has real consequences for Floridians. While GOP leaders are entangled in power struggles, critical issues like**healthcare, education, and economic recovery are sidelined.** Our communities deserve representatives who prioritize people over party politics. **It's time to move beyond the chaos and infighting that have paralyzed progress.** With your support, we can champion policies that directly benefit our communities and restore integrity to our representation.

name: Ritchie
subject: I’m not your typical Congressman, Peter.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Torres For Congress PO Box 580303, Bronx, NY 10458
body:
| [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102202644/528411821/1654258770?refcode=header-img&amount=25&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjE2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMmE2NzVkYi1kZmViLWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9X9f1gLm7cxfTiBmjo3qtlAebG1V20qHFVLHrUJKbq0=)
---
When people think about what Congress members look like, it’s not me:

**I’m not your typical Congressman.
I don’t have a college degree.
I was raised in public housing in the Bronx.**

I grew up as a poor kid in our district — and my lived experience allows me to represent my district & fight for what we need.

But here’s the thing, Peter: No one tells you how expensive it is to run for Congress.

**Thanks to a grassroots base of people who believe in this fight and believe in giving their money to campaigns that can make a real difference, I am in Washington fighting for you everyday.**

[I don’t do a second of call-time, so I have to ask: Will you chip in just $3 towards tonight’s mid-month goal to help me continue this fight? Meeting goals like tonight’s will keep us on track to save our people-powered momentum and continue the fight for our Democracy:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102202645/528411822/-864556716?amount=3&refcode=RTFC_20250215_EMDF_E2_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjE2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMmE2NzVkYi1kZmViLWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9X9f1gLm7cxfTiBmjo3qtlAebG1V20qHFVLHrUJKbq0=) If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | **[CONTRIBUTE $3 »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102202654/528411832/-864556716?amount=3&express_lane=true&refcode=RTFC_20250215_EMDF_E2_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjE2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMmE2NzVkYi1kZmViLWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9X9f1gLm7cxfTiBmjo3qtlAebG1V20qHFVLHrUJKbq0=)**

It means a lot to have you on my team, Peter.

_Ritchie_
| | Paid for by Torres For Congress PO Box 580303, Bronx, NY 10458
www.torres.nyc

Ritchie Torres represents the future of the Democratic Party. Raised by a single mother in public housing, Ritchie personally understands the hardships that working families endure. At age 25, Ritchie made history as the youngest elected official to serve on the New York City Council. He fought tirelessly for the working families of The Bronx, and now, he's fighting for those families and for progressive values in Congress. clean_body: | = When people think about what Congress members look like, it’s not me: I’m not your typical Congressman. I don’t have a college degree. I was raised in public housing in the Bronx. I grew up as a poor kid in our district — and my lived experience allows me to represent my district & fight for what we need. But here’s the thing, Peter: No one tells you how expensive it is to run for Congress. Thanks to a grassroots base of people who believe in this fight and believe in giving their money to campaigns that can make a real difference, I am in Washington fighting for you everyday. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = = = It means a lot to have you on my team, Peter. Ritchie | | Paid for by Torres For Congress PO Box 580303, Bronx, NY 10458 www.torres.nyc Ritchie Torres represents the future of the Democratic Party. Raised by a single mother in public housing, Ritchie personally understands the hardships that working families endure. At age 25, Ritchie made history as the youngest elected official to serve on the New York City Council. He fought tirelessly for the working families of The Bronx, and now, he's fighting for those families and for progressive values in Congress. He is the first openly gay Afro Latino ever elected to Congress. Ritchie is a champion for progressive policies to fight poverty and to improve the lives of families just like the one he was raised in. Peter, please, consider making a =. Happy Saturday,
Peter!

We’ll keep this short and sweet so you can get back to enjoying your
weekend with your friends and family.

We have less than 16 hours left to reach our $30,000 mid-February goal —
and we’re still $4,992 away from closing the gap.

The far-right is ramping up its attacks, and the Trump administration is
working harder than ever to gut our democracy and build his unjust and
corrupt empire. We need every resource to push back and protect the rights
we’ve fought so hard for.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20250215-movement.p4k8EI&refcode=em20250215-1229&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=1229%2E342411%2EUe6JhT ]Can we count on you to chip in $3 to ensure our Squad’s
fundraising is on track to support the many plans our Squad has to fight
back against the Trump administration and his billionaire cronies?

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20250215-movement.p4k8EI&refcode=em20250215-1229&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=1229%2E342411%2EUe6JhT ]Contribute ››

Together,

Alexandria, Ayanna, Ilhan, Rashida

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.squadvictoryfund.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1229%2E342411%2EUe6JhT

Paid for by The Squad Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated as follows: 8.33% to each of Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, Rooted in Community Leadership PAC, Politivist PAC, and For the Children PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

# [#] Friends,

From fighting NY’s outrageous Congestion Tax, and restoring the SALT deduction, to lowering property taxes and making childcare more affordable, to ending utility price hikes and reducing the cost of housing, to defending reproductive freedom, fighting for the hardworking people of Jersey is always on Josh’s menu.

Our mid-month deadline is tonight! Chip in $ to help us build up our grassroots strength and make sure Josh’s message reaches voters across our state. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/JcKdD-Q5sEmr2Bb9s_L8tg2?amount=]

name: Mandela Barnes
subject: The Senate map just got shaken up
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Long Run PAC, P
body:
Democratic Senator Tina Smith announced yesterday that she will not seek reelection
#
Friend — A few weeks back my team told you we were already working on our strategy to support changemaking candidates in battleground districts in next year’s midterms.
**We just got a big shakeup.**
Democratic Senator Tina Smith has announced that she will not seek reelection, _meaning that there will be an open race for Minnesota's Senate seat in 2026._
This changes our math. We knew we had to defend seats in Michigan, Georgia, and New Hampshire, but now with an open race in Minnesota, the GOP could have a real shot at flipping this seat and adding to their majority.
That’s the last thing we need with Trump and Elon Musk continuing their illegal takeover of the federal government and Republicans in Congress sitting idly by. If want to flip the Senate, Minnesota is an **absolute must-win.**
So here’s our ask for you today: Help us get ahead of the GOP in this crucial Senate race.
We can bet that they will be calling around to their corporate mega-donors following this news. We have to counter them, starting now.
[**Make a contribution to The Long Run today and help us support the eventual Democratic nominee in Minnesota. We are committing to helping win this seat and taking back the Senate.**](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=longrunpac-em-feb25.oEbnj0&refcode=em250215-1-240&t=1&akid=5485%2E196590%2EcALP7Q)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._

name: Johnny O
subject: ICYMI: Johnny O Tells Trump to Keep His Hands Off Social Security
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Johnny O for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/joc-2025?refcode=1.3 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/joc-2025?refcode=1.3]
WBALTV: Olszewski “Pushes Back” Against Trump Administration Policies

The rallies outside U.S. federal agencies targeted by President Trump, Elon Musk and their DOGE team continue and this week I headed to the Social Security headquarters in Woodlawn. WBAL-TV covered our protest, where we were joined not only by dedicated federal workers, but those who stand to lose the benefits they rightfully earned. [https://www.wbaltv.com/article/maryland-congressional-leaders-push-back-trump-policies/63744678]
These two things aren’t important. They are sacrosanct.
I will keep fighting to protect our civil servants and the 71.6 million Americans who rely on Social Security – the most efficiently run insurance company in the country – every day.
We need to keep up the drumbeat. Forward this message to a friend, or contribute to help me amplify our message.

**We CAN'T just stand by as the GOP takes direct aim at PBS and NPR,** so we've launched an urgent Live Petition to PROVE Democrats support funding these all-American institutions. We need 5,000 more signatures by midnight -- **please, don't delay:**

name: Lila Rose
subject: We can finally defund Planned Parenthood—if we act now
body:
Did you know that Planned Parenthood received $699 million dollars from taxpayers in the last reported year?

| | _Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this[link](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/u/HsIPncOM8E1FV-RGkPX2eZCcCoE-aF73s6-9GvqrD6-omxhqRlleSPAlUdpBLfEMmsxA0aAO7kYfWuTYLTO7rd3Et39BG24-zDBDBmkopTH7UriA-lUia4rU5xtgSJ9QIK8HEYq-SwZEHz_PXylnHHkf8IVvIVz0zBhtWU14rM66Mt1T9zFRbQHR/gS_NQnylpiTnPKzlaFybwQ6m2l5ytfhi/6)_
---
[ ](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/sFjXEAVPoo7BQVQDR4GkH7lvnRKPGKmndUeP7GDuY2vEDXZc8-xrY4mQfZHbuxKMam9B4brvLfoub2FbtFKu_eQQ2ADrfUBm7ZEy8JDE3dLd-4NKvJuW8cVSVvDB0MBf2o6YF5y2LB8ea93s-yOlLAnouTpn3yarlOqcMCG0BnaXb4EBLZCyInIKUQiAGCDToghLYHz2czDixDBvoBhxxyi5sjS96KMFOZJVGtc7T6V2lK-TOFMjYeKhe2fk1fWNQMwSWMkwpPbl_pgSHjBkBcSMaaFw1_hhavSS5dNUOfGYnvPFnJPYCWhc02i5CkLKnCMMgSRkC65XA9nONYfDHO3IKGBOFTsBr86LqsSYOBGS-NAvpMjmRvgIau0dvup986nQztANmROwxJ94xlIrjlCkFf_0qPsQ4LdttFgx5vIReW0B1qWg5IMLWN4/ITkzKZ6EG91sbmW3an3wrmTKSoGOAp0N/6)
#
Friend of Life,

Did you know that Planned Parenthood received **$699 million dollars** from taxpayers in the last reported year? In fact, the abortion corporation has received billions of dollars from the government over the past few decades!

Based on reporting from its 2022-2023 annual report, Planned Parenthood killed approximately 1,076 preborn babies every day, 45 every hour, and one every 80 seconds. Its annual killing rate has increased by 20,000 since the previous reported year.

Thankfully, national leaders are beginning to take notice.

President Trump has chosen Elon Musk to head the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), a new government agency that aims to reduce waste and spending, including potentially pulling federal funding from Planned Parenthood.

Last fall, Elon Musk co-authored an op-ed, published by the Wall Street Journal (WSJ), which said:

_" ...DOGE will help end federal overspending by taking aim at the $500 billion plus in annual federal expenditures that are unauthorized by Congress or being used in ways that Congress never intended, from $535 million a year to the Corporation for Public Broadcasting and $1.5 billion for grants to international organizations to nearly $300 million to progressive groups like Planned Parenthood."_

This is incredibly hopeful news, Friend of Life, but it's not final. [ ](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/Y_mKkNSIfixNLFxydMUD7yM0A6BQgIzYcDETzGBi_IxtKzmd1GEMYdxoEJZgFfm15AYoc6hFmv3LpixXp0oB5ZxL9ML_QsniI_G8AJmnLtaUnI8x88ZYPVzfqzOC9uVRMRqqRW0qlJGJcOMiSj7RwIOWCfwzixp1afLokuOjOxKDPLqZQ1XX8lk-Ov-6qHFOSDZXOcR9jwFlxNulNbEVYB2LVYzQsPt2rAg1KxOzPlj3SAmkbb735oEwYGrIKL_NW-ldyPuwzoJPxHPR_MTSZiHSwjlp4oKBx-qd_0J9yA0SHL5le4zCHtBXfkUc3QOj_7GvZZXDvD9rrLbwJT4djsb0PmpT3JacYcExldL7KJ_MGAUje07t5V7CEyWv7P9WHgWbDaLVxINk_A8-fXL_jQtsoVfUyp1dAld7E68mDe_hCl9uDQgKmqogc9o/U5TuWLaDonXOuC4qNKWt9lM0rdLn-8K2/6)
The fact that more people are recognizing our tax dollars should not go to such an evil corporation is surely due to the persistent activism of pro-life work to expose the horrific truth about Planned Parenthood.

To keep pressure on the Trump administration, Live Action is spearheading a petition that will go to these national leaders, demanding the idea of defunding Planned Parenthood become a reality. **Sign your name here, and share it with your friends and family:[https://www.liveaction.org/end-taxpayer-dollars-to-planned-parenthood](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/2We-RMwvW3zO02HIalwvulj_sT-FyvyZyKSvGomsjgfI0oT9FSkW0as9cEuLdziRezx0w7joAA2av5LeJodVqC0e-dL1q61Ebc33mTm2QVDOr83wwjEl6e41pl4ziv73n8tOG0-_DVfCREgNSJI4xvO8CXnj7jzCyZLzIlXtCYweXJ6_iRrj6OxBEeO7WvsvKhqpiBBN2jDfX1ojhdxoJBz2LW7Z7vN0aryiF-LYMv8PUWYe-iVutMdBrCzY6FkGclP6ZPDXGUlyXnPWAc4966g5FHsQ_kBO3S_f503oJJsTxVPPfOuJFfkDTbQQRlph_C3inhRXx6PEPK0lByXJ1xmi-yaqo5VPRE561kLM0rZlSwT1N3slt5Vo0gFed4eiM89OzKgpRnqHwYblkvqqlPaqyW3pAyRunuRbqja1IjrB6RjOKoeY3BI-Axc/U_Fsv-x1AGCJmghzhJIiyS8i5NNm6JRg/6)**

Defunding Planned Parenthood will require more than DOGE's involvement. We need the HHS leadership (recently confirmed Robert F. Kennedy, Jr.) to get on board, as well as obvious leadership from President Trump.

Musk, Kennedy, and Trump need to hear affirmation from the American people. They need to know we wholeheartedly support the defunding of Planned Parenthood!

Everyone needs to know: Over 1,000 abortions take place via Planned Parenthood every day in America. 1,000 children gone, forever, under the sickening guise of "care, no matter what."

We must put an end to this atrocity, beginning with the removal of federal funding. **Sign your name to declare to these national leaders that you see the value in every human life, you will speak for the most vulnerable among us who cannot speak for themselves, and you do not want any of your hard-earned dollars to go to Planned Parenthood:[https://www.liveaction.org/end-taxpayer-dollars-to-planned-parenthood](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/p7V6nDa3aBWBI7hCaOwdXH8_-7J4LY1kgFOmXBf-DXc5guv8ztEyaxAhC_V3J0dX6IQDfgcvPWTqVq_jsuQU1BYqnPszQsyCEG3lXPMXW7OgM1jKItkFFGyykk5wByC0ad5XLX29gM99XZDRJEv0NOO9-S35BObNw7ZpQ0R9CL4A0ks76r8HdNmfja2lsRqPRCxBWj6IjOKXfQD7Vq2V-jLIdsQFoUrFyYE_sxI_qoEh1jOTm2eqMH3tbA7I2qt-zfZxgsklyHXMehiDfCQH7tiOgwdZFPPhzx0efEWA6pyT_6KT5Imli0rRCQcrre0Meu-pCHb_lSqMBA6h_0gfJ8skLIn-ceAuH2HPDfdYwY-Gx8pBXwFcivg4mGRAqMK9fPHBtmMlexb0_V5Cfq2UXu7xrwv53uKvOa3mpZ2Gp2gfh3aJThNvvqaMR7k/6L9dd-uWCCHaevPoLRHJMNZ_3GRdEF32/6)**

Thank you for standing with us in this concrete way to show America's leaders that we do NOT want to fund the killing of America's most vulnerable.

For Life,

Lila Rose
**Founder and President**

[](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/4TogclMWqV67dPE9XXhpFti9wfeLAd1Rua2khkcLClcKi0x2P81NA7WnmFiWCiFWJOLy1r0jM55CLtR9nfy8jWMeHAWGa8PiFtUAFQNHKRfUyCtsbIAuWzUHnSvk_3g3MYrpvDJwr-V7BX4aBCfqNZV0c8jAifkO3wLPdzm7jdvr81VL0FY4hqkolNj52rL1YQqK-acF_g8Y1hbpoiSyMxyg7ANzl0LhAN_giFoMNHDcKkOTVRlZEztjkYDhvfzbaXrV21DAeJv2GYRNOf9aq1J9bQf8dpQvmpMCdrwlmcvWR7TH-e5eIcdIO0Rve8nGAybGY59rcNdFRDQfKwptmVaEtUpMiU-HSBbcBZZEhmHWIM47Nu30B5GljV2QYtRO7OsLTy_g666dUNrGkLkEBAgwar_1np4837ixZ3wxr7G2OUb39orVR-HSWhc/aQmkbh5whdGg95WWN2sxTKP9tXhE9JbM/6)

2200 Wilson Blvd, Suite 102, PMB 111 Arlington, VA 22201
#
[Unsubscribe from messages sponsored by Live Action.](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/u/10fEHYuCq-9Y3O5PV3jAnVQIKPQ0dPCPb48bFjHzKR5f3p00evKrWeh5angVwDau1hEPgJrWYaM5XAO4wFM2FGBufphvMZA6ifJL2viVJ7Et-YbbPrVTu76H42kECXXVzJU-ZXt79okEOn0FAygmk7jVyjuNqWB_T0hjKU3nVaYRhq_XpOF-B7rEFHH_FsPXloh27LwlKuSVFAGOdFSfD4M5leCVTKKm2w/c3cOpQDe5pesVTqSIcSpyEnEQe2N1mK-/6)

[Unsubscribe from all communications](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/u/HsIPncOM8E1FV-RGkPX2eZCcCoE-aF73s6-9GvqrD6-omxhqRlleSPAlUdpBLfEMmsxA0aAO7kYfWuTYLTO7rd3Et39BG24-zDBDBmkopTH7UriA-lUia4rU5xtgSJ9QIK8HEYq-SwZEHz_PXylnHHkf8IVvIVz0zBhtWU14rM66Mt1T9zFRbQHR/gS_NQnylpiTnPKzlaFybwQ6m2l5ytfhi/6).

name: Jocelyn Benson
subject: Here’s what I’m hearing on the ground
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR GOVERNOR
body:
I am running to be that Governor.
# [](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/203?t=1&akid=105%2E181023%2EAq-9fU)
Team, our campaign to make government work for everyone is growing by the day.
Thanks to grassroots supporters like you, I’ve been able to travel across Michigan and meet with voters to discuss the future of our state.
Here’s what they tell me: Michiganders want a Governor who will fight every single day to ensure that their families have a fair shot at success.
**I am running to be that Governor.**
I will fight to bring transparent leadership, safe schools and neighborhoods, and good education and childcare to Michigan.
But I can’t do it without your help. Our very first mid-quarter fundraising goal of this campaign expires at midnight tonight, and what we’re able to raise before then will determine the strength of our campaign.
[**That’s why I’m personally asking: Can you chip in a donation before our fundraising deadline at 11:59 P.M. tonight to help us reach our goal and continue growing this campaign?**](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q12025.B7hgkZ&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_MQG_AC_20250215_PM1_V1_S1&t=2&akid=105%2E181023%2EAq-9fU)

name: Adam Schiff
subject: How you can help flip the Senate.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/62?t=1001&akid=8299%2E5257348%2EkH8N-A ]Adam Schiff for Senate

Derek — Last
year, because of your support, Californians elected me to the United
States Senate.

But your support went much further than our race in CA: You sent
grassroots contributions to candidates in tough races across the country —
and you helped make a difference in some key elections.

Ruben Gallego in Arizona. Elissa Slotkin in Michigan. Angela Alsobrooks in
Maryland. Jacky Rosen in Nevada. The list goes on.

Our work to support Democrats running for the Senate continues, and is
more urgent at this moment in time:

I was recently named Vice Chair of the Democratic Senate Campaign
Committee — and I’m ready to roll up my sleeves and do all I can to help
Democrats regain the Senate.

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/2907?c0=q12025_abs_em.OeZ3Kx&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20250215_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1004&akid=8299%2E5257348%2EkH8N-A ]Please chip in to help us flip control of the Senate and win tough
races from coast to coast.

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation will go through immediately:

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/2142?c0=q12025_abs_em.UcG9Ic&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20250215_PM1_V1_S1&email=&amount=10&t=1005&akid=8299%2E5257348%2EkH8N-A ]EXPRESS DONATE

name: Nellie Pou
subject: Following up
body:
I just wanted to say thank you

| |
---
Friend, My team reached out a couple of days ago with a rundown on the dynamics of our race. I know we're not used to NJ-09 being a battleground, but there's no denying that it is. 2026 will be our toughest election yet, and while I know Mike Johnson and the NRCC are ready to throw everything they've got at us — I'm not afraid. **I'm not afraid because I know I have you on my team, friend.** Thanks to you and all of our supporters, we made history when I won last fall and became the first Latina to represent New Jersey in Congress. We surpassed our January online fundraising goal in the face of NRCC attacks, and we're on track to hit our February goal too. **[With a few hours left before midnight, we're only $1,018 short of our mid-month February online fundraising goal. ### About Nellie Pou

clean_body: I just wanted to say thank you | | Friend, My team reached out a couple of days ago with a rundown on the dynamics of our race. I know we're not used to NJ-09 being a battleground, but there's no denying that it is. 2026 will be our toughest election yet, and while I know Mike Johnson and the NRCC are ready to throw everything they've got at us — I'm not afraid. I'm not afraid because I know I have you on my team, friend. Thanks to you and all of our supporters, we made history when I won last fall and became the first Latina to represent New Jersey in Congress. We surpassed our January online fundraising goal in the face of NRCC attacks, and we're on track to hit our February goal too. = | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately. Thank you so much for your support. I remain incredibly grateful for each and every one of you who are standing with us, and helping us fight back. This battle will be tough, but I know we have what it takes to win. — Nellie Pou | | | ---|---|--- About Nellie Pou Nellie Pou is the first Latina to represent New Jersey in Congress, and will be a target for the NRCC in 2026. As a mother and grandmother, Nellie knows what New Jersey families are facing and will continue to bring down costs while creating good-paying jobs. Nellie has dedicated her entire life to the community that raised her, and in Congress she will do the same. | = | = ---|--- Follow Nellie | | | ---|---|--- Checks may also be mailed to: Nellie Pou for Congress - PO BOX 2696, Paterson, NJ, 07509 Paid for by Nellie Pou for Congress Email is a critical way to stay in touch with our supporters, but if you wish to unsubscribe, you may do so . Dear Rachel Maddow, THANK YOU for your unwavering commitment to truth and your courageous journalism. ******Your dedication to holding Donald Trump accountable and exposing the facts has been critical in safeguarding our democracy.** You have consistently brought attention to critical issues, providing clarity amidst confusion. **** Your insightful analyses and fearless reporting have empowered citizens to stay informed and engaged. In the face of personal attacks and attempts to undermine your credibility, you have remained resolute. Even after being labeled as the "enemy of the people" by Donald Trump, you continued to speak truth to power. Your work has not only informed but also inspired us to remain vigilant in standing up to MAGA forces. For this, we extend our deepest appreciation. Thank you for your unwavering dedication to the truth. Sincerely, Peter

---

GOP Rep. Maria Salazar **[tore apart former USAID Director Andrew Natsios](https://substack.com/redirect/dcd35282-3950-4333-bf4a-e4138f6877d0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** during a House hearing on wasteful spending of taxpayer dollars on woke foreign programs.
"$25,000 for diversity recruitment events... What is a 'diversity recruitment event' in the middle of the jungle?!!" Salazar pressed Natsios about DEI programs in Peru.
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/dcd35282-3950-4333-bf4a-e4138f6877d0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/dcd35282-3950-4333-bf4a-e4138f6877d0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Natsios admitted that politics has diverted USAID from its original intended mission.
"Oh!! Politics!" Salazar exclaimed. "But then Elon Musk comes and tries to clean politics and go back to the original mission of the program, correct?"
Natsios explained that USAID could be redirected in a more conservative direction if a conservative leader was appointed. "The USAID staff does what the political appointees tell them to do,... they don't initiate these things!"
"So it came from the White House?! You are telling me that during the Biden administration, USAID lost it's course?! Yes or no?" Salazar pressed further.
Watch the heated exchange right here:
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/dcd35282-3950-4333-bf4a-e4138f6877d0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
# _PB Originals:_ **What Happens When You Ask Liberals To Fund INSANE DEI Programs**
We asked liberals in Washington D.C. to support USAID's crazy DEI programs to see what would happen...
They actually support this stuff?!!
"We're raising money for a transgender opera in Columbia, can you donate today?"
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/0366e734-0de7-4933-bdce-197618db1c31?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/0366e734-0de7-4933-bdce-197618db1c31?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *

# **SHOCKING REPORT: CHINA ATTACKS U.S. WATER SUPPLY**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/62d485bc-b564-4b41-8ccc-595f369b6d87?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_**FBI's Urgent Warning Has Been All Over The News**_
Chinese hackers are coming for the water supply in your hometown.
Attacking city water systems, biding their time and waiting for China's signal to strike. Experts warn that Russia and Iran are also targeting attacks on our city water systems.
Overflowing, damaging and poisoning the lifegiving systems that we trust to keep us safe.
Like it or not, your local tap water may be our country's weakest link.
Deteriorating infrastructure. Systems years beyond their expiration dates. Negligent corporations. And God-knows-what in the water we drink.
We're up against arsenic, plastics, chlorine, forever chemicals, hormone-altering drug residue and a host of other contaminants.
Listen, I'm not trying to be the messenger of doom...
**But protecting the water supply in your home is more important than ever.**
If you don't take steps to be prepared, you and your family could end up vulnerable. Forced to choose between drinking poisoned, toxic or dirty water, or going without water altogether.
It sounds harsh... But the truth is that even good people with good intentions can make serious mistakes.
Mistakes like... **Getting filters that claim to purify your water, but don't prove it.**
If a company doesn't have certifications from a third-party lab, show lab tests or reveal how many gallons were tested... that's a bad sign. After a couple gallons, most water filters on the market stop working for scary contaminants and start passing them into your water.
**Or simply using a pitcher in the fridge... with slapshot filtration power and limited capacity.**
Most pitchers hold a couple cups of water at a time. Not nearly enough to drink, cook and clean in an emergency. You'll run out in no time, just when your life depends on it.
Well we have great news to share. Folks have been leaving Facebook messages asking about our just-released...

**[The Ultimate Water Filtration System!](https://substack.com/redirect/62d485bc-b564-4b41-8ccc-595f369b6d87?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
**But hurry - we have limited stock, and these Ultimate Water Filtration Systems are first-come first-served. We only have a small number of units to go around.**

clean_body: | = Team, We set a goal of receiving 50,000 grassroots donations before our first mid-quarter fundraising deadline of the year at midnight tonight — and now, we’re less than 3,661 gifts away from reaching it! = | = Donald Trump’s work to carry out his far-right agenda is underway. He’s staffing his administration with unqualified loyalists, issuing executive orders directly from the Project 2025 playbook, and directing his allies to pursue legislation that would give his wealthy allies a massive tax break at the expense of working people. Senate Republicans like Susan Collins and Thom Tillis refuse to stand up to Trump, which is why we need to vote them out in 2026. This fight won’t be easy. Trump and his wealthy allies aren’t going to waste a second doing whatever it takes to expand their GOP Senate majority. That’s why we’re counting on folks like you to help us reach this fundraising goal and ensure that we can fight back. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = Democrats in your area can make all the difference in our fight to win the Senate for Democrats. name: Heartland Patriots Alert
subject: What it takes to run for office as a normal person
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
Read more »
#
Peter,
Founder of Heartland Patriots, Lucas Kunce, shared an update on Substack recently about his experience on the campaign trail as a normal person — along with seven tips for anyone considering a run for office.
**Read an excerpt below, then please consider following Lucas on Substack.**
_* * *_
_1\. Be prepared to lose._
_I believed that I could win every race I entered, even though the odds were long. And while that was important to me, particularly since I was asking so many people to support the campaign, I don’t think it’s a requirement when running for office. That may sound controversial (as might a few things I say later), but I think it’s true._
_You can run knowing you are going to lose and never believing you have a chance. Some people do it for fame or attention, because they want to become a TV pundit, for vanity, because they don’t think the incumbent should get a free pass, because they have something to prove, or whatever. If you want to run for those reasons, there is nothing stopping you and history shows you can succeed._
_I’ll talk about motivations for running in number 4 below, but I am saving that for now because I think it’s really important to understand that whether your seat is a sure thing for you because you cleared the primary field in a safe seat, whether it’s winnable but contested, or whether it’s a long shot, you need to be prepared both mentally and financially to lose. Way more people lose running for office than win (incumbent advantage, multiple people running for a seat, etc.). If you can’t survive a loss, you shouldn’t do it. Which brings me to number two._
_2\. Have personal financial security._
_Remember, there is a good chance you will lose, so you have to have a way to survive that, especially if you have a family. This is probably the number one reason why normal people don’t run for office and why most people who run for office, particularly higher office, are rich, are from political families (usually meaning they’re rich), have someone rich backing them up, married rich, or have family wealth._
_Because money is everything in politics, both for running and paying for a campaign, and also for surviving personally during a campaign. Every time I’ve talked to a normal person who is thinking about running for office, this is the thing we talk about the most. It’s the biggest barrier to normal people running, winning, and then representing us in Congress. It’s a reason why so many sell out in their pursuit of lower offices in order to climb the corrupt political ladder. Because that’s the path that’s ready built for a normal person. The system sucks._
_But there are ways._
_You can choose an office that doesn’t take as much time and/or money to run for. I’ll talk about choosing your office later. You can be self-employed or own a business. You can have a job that lets you work while you run._
_A note on that: there are a decent number of people who have jobs that let them focus a lot of time on running while still paying them. Technically, that could be a campaign finance contribution from your employer if they let you do that, so there is risk involved, but it’s a way. It is also a great reminder of what an advantage those incumbents who never do their actual elected jobs, or even those who do do their jobs most of the time, have while running for office. They can campaign full time while getting paid by us whereas if a normal person’s job lets them do it that’s a campaign contribution (which probably exceeds contribution limits). Again, the system sucks and it’s built for rich people and incumbents._
_For most of the time I was running, you could pay yourself out of campaign funds in a federal race, under certain circumstances, after filing, with limitations, and you would get skewered by the media and probably in tv ads, so people didn’t do it. New rules came into effect[last year that will hopefully make it better for federal candidates,](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/85878?t=1&akid=8770%2E614464%2Exh83ZM) but won’t do anything for local candidates and who knows what the media or opponents will do with it._
_Then, there’s the old fashioned way, cut costs as much as possible and try to scrape by, which is what I did. Here are some details on how I got through each race, which should be good information for you no matter what office you are running for..._
**[READ MORE + SUBSCRIBE](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/85879?t=2&akid=8770%2E614464%2Exh83ZM)**

_**— A message from Heartland Patriots —**_ Electing strong leaders who will stand up to the corruption and chaos happening in our government right now has never been more important. **[Will you rush a donation today to support real Heartland Patriots in tough races across the country?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/85880?refcode=em20250215-8770&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=8770%2E614464%2Exh83ZM)** **[DONATE NOW](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/85880?refcode=em20250215-8770&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=8770%2E614464%2Exh83ZM)**
---

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hp-email-footer?refcode=em20250215-8770-footer&t=5&refcode2=8770_614464_xh83ZM&akid=8770%2E614464%2Exh83ZM)**
---

Have personal financial security. Remember, there is a good chance you will lose, so you have to have a way to survive that, especially if you have a family. This is probably the number one reason why normal people don’t run for office and why most people who run for office, particularly higher office, are rich, are from political families (usually meaning they’re rich), have someone rich backing them up, married rich, or have family wealth. Because money is everything in politics, both for running and paying for a campaign, and also for surviving personally during a campaign. Every time I’ve talked to a normal person who is thinking about running for office, this is the thing we talk about the most. It’s the biggest barrier to normal people running, winning, and then representing us in Congress. It’s a reason why so many sell out in their pursuit of lower offices in order to climb the corrupt political ladder. Because that’s the path that’s ready built for a normal person. The system sucks. But there are ways. You can choose an office that doesn’t take as much time and/or money to run for. I’ll talk about choosing your office later. You can be self-employed or own a business. You can have a job that lets you work while you run. A note on that: there are a decent number of people who have jobs that let them focus a lot of time on running while still paying them. Technically, that could be a campaign finance contribution from your employer if they let you do that, so there is risk involved, but it’s a way. It is also a great reminder of what an advantage those incumbents who never do their actual elected jobs, or even those who do do their jobs most of the time, have while running for office. They can campaign full time while getting paid by us whereas if a normal person’s job lets them do it that’s a campaign contribution (which probably exceeds contribution limits). Again, the system sucks and it’s built for rich people and incumbents. For most of the time I was running, you could pay yourself out of campaign funds in a federal race, under certain circumstances, after filing, with limitations, and you would get skewered by the media and probably in tv ads, so people didn’t do it. New rules came into effect but won’t do anything for local candidates and who knows what the media or opponents will do with it. Then, there’s the old fashioned way, cut costs as much as possible and try to scrape by, which is what I did. Here are some details on how I got through each race, which should be good information for you no matter what office you are running for... — A message from Heartland Patriots — Electing strong leaders who will stand up to the corruption and chaos happening in our government right now has never been more important. ** | | Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce.com). Not authorized by any federal candidate or candidate committee.

subject: Beyond the Chaos: Navigating This Moment With Integrity, Together
body:
Reaction isn't leadership. We are in a fight not just for policies, but for the fundamental shape of the future. Here's why I'm writing again--and how we build what comes next.

| |
---|---|---

# [Beyond the Chaos: Navigating This Moment With Integrity, Together](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=157220837&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU3MjIwODM3LCJpYXQiOjE3Mzk2NjA5MzIsImV4cCI6MTc0MjI1MjkzMiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.aYbXUMG8Nw1qmNoNAJRMI0ZDn8E0d-09nmJfDZOsEfk)
### Reaction isn't leadership. We are in a fight not just for policies, but for the fundamental shape of the future. Here's why I'm writing again--and how we build what comes next.
| | [Melissa Chaudhry](https://substack.com/@melissa4congress)
---
| Feb 15
---
| [](https://substack.com/@melissa4congress)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=157220837&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU3MjIwODM3LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTczOTY2MDkzMiwiZXhwIjoxNzQyMjUyOTMyLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzI2MDExMSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ZQI2mOg-oJ6wt4lyF3--Ri8V0dqwUQpeM6vUCUfqpTc&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=157220837&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU3MjIwODM3LCJpYXQiOjE3Mzk2NjA5MzIsImV4cCI6MTc0MjI1MjkzMiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.aYbXUMG8Nw1qmNoNAJRMI0ZDn8E0d-09nmJfDZOsEfk&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=157220837&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU3MjIwODM3LCJpYXQiOjE3Mzk2NjA5MzIsImV4cCI6MTc0MjI1MjkzMiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.aYbXUMG8Nw1qmNoNAJRMI0ZDn8E0d-09nmJfDZOsEfk)
---
| | [](https://substack..-Suo9nVuAkzCnZvvei-113SqBFZdLpxAM33kATXjORA?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/melissa4congress/p/beyond-the-chaos-navigating-this?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Friends...
The world is accelerating toward a crossroads.
We're not just dealing with political dysfunction--we are facing a **deep, systemic crisis** of governance, sustainability, and social trust. The institutions we have long relied on are fraying. The guardrails that once seemed unshakable are being tested--and in some cases, dismantled in real time.
But these fractures are not new. They are the latest manifestation of patterns that history has shown us before: the entrenchment of power in fewer hands, the scapegoating of marginalized communities, the erosion of democratic norms under the guise of "order" and "efficiency." The dismantling of USAID, the purge of funding from anything touching diversity, environmental justice, or women's empowerment, the ideological crackdown on immigrants and international students--none of this is accidental. It is a **blueprint** , and we would be wise to recognize it as such.
As some of my international friends - including Germans... \- have observed, **Americans are still in the denial stage.** The consolidation of power we are witnessing, particularly through figures like Elon Musk--who now wields more direct influence over global infrastructure than the sitting U.S. president--has no real precedent in modern democracy. The most powerful person on the planet today grew up under apartheid, benefited from apartheid, and, in plain sight, threw **two unmistakable Nazi salutes at the U.S. presidential inauguration** on January 20. If we are still pretending this is normal, we are already losing the plot.
The truth is, we are in a fight not just for policies, but for **the fundamental shape of the future.** And while it is tempting to react--every day--to the latest outrage or authoritarian maneuver, **reaction is not leadership.** And we**must** lead ourselves.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=157220837&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU3MjIwODM3LCJpYXQiOjE3Mzk2NjA5MzIsImV4cCI6MTc0MjI1MjkzMiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.aYbXUMG8Nw1qmNoNAJRMI0ZDn8E0d-09nmJfDZOsEfk)
### **Why I Ran in 2024 --And Why I'm Running Again in 2026**
I ran for Congress in 2024 because I saw the cracks forming long before election season. I saw the dysfunction, the failures of imagination, and the widening gap between **what is politically possible and what is actually needed for human and planetary survival.**
And here's the thing: just in our district alone, **tens of thousands of people saw it too.** Despite running a campaign with almost no advertising, on a shoestring grassroots budget, in the shortest election timeline possible, over 90,000 people voted for the vision, courage, and principle I brought. That is **proof of concept.** That is evidence that people are looking for **something different --someone who speaks plainly, who isn't owned, and who actually understands what's at stake.**
So _yes, I 'm running again in 2026_. **But this work is bigger than an election.**
If all I wanted was a seat in Congress, I could twist myself into the shape of a "traditional" candidate. I could self-censor, manipulate people's emotions, bow to the powerful, take corrupt money, toe the party line, and play it safe. But **winning for the sake of winning is not the point.** Never has been, and never will be.
I have always believed that our real task is **to see further --to build the systems, communities, and alternative structures that will allow us to endure, outlast, and eventually replace the failing ones.**
That is why I haven't been publishing here for a while. I needed to see what this moment would bring, and take a deep, long breath and figure out how best to approach it - what will best serve you, us, and the future. Reacting to attacks, while necessary, isn't sufficient for long-term success. What we need **isn 't just more analysis.** We need **strategy, resilience, and a realignment toward long-term thinking.** We're fighting battles on dozens of fronts, but we need a strategy not just to win, but to _outlast_ the war.
[Give a gift subscription](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9tZWxpc3NhNGNvbmdyZXNzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.UmSwq2FYVCxsF7jIrAXn2dJoKKcA4lD9koFdEysmPEM?&gift=true)
### **This Substack Is Back --And Here's What to Expect**
This is why I'm back, and where I bring this work to you.
Some of what I'll write about here will touch on my campaign--what it means to run for Congress as a principled outsider, the fights worth having, the ways we can **pull the future into the present instead of getting dragged backward.** Rest assured that I am, and will always be, on the side of principle, courage, standing up for the most vulnerable, and creating a world that truly works for all - and when you need an ally in any of those fights, you can trust me. Never hesitate to reach out.
But some of what I publish here will go beyond politics, into work that is, or I hope will be, immediately relevant to your daily life.
I want to talk about:
* **How we build resilient communities and economies outside of broken systems.**
* **How we recognize historical patterns in real-time and navigate them wisely.**
* **How we maintain clarity, courage, and adaptability in a time of upheaval.**
* **How we build lives, families, and futures that are worthy of the world we hope to see.**

This is where we do that work. If that sounds like something you want to be part of, stick around. The next post will lay out **what 's coming next.**
And if you want to see me in person--if you want to talk about this moment, this movement, and the way forward--come see me speak on **February 22nd at MAPS (Muslim Association of Puget Sound),** where I'll be at the AMAC Banquet with Mehdi Hasan. You can register [here](https://substack.com/redirect/e8001746-830e-4ccd-99d1-4f0ef5f07ceb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
I look forward to seeing you there - we need to be having these conversations **together.**
There's work to do, opportunities to build, and a future to save. Let's begin.
With clarity and resolve,
**Melissa**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/64ff9286-f802-4bae-ab39-9d8920667df6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
